    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - ## Entry 80
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:28:46.403Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:28:46.403Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "request_user_input",
    "arguments": "{\"questions\":[{\"header\":\"L1 scope\",\"id\":\"level1_scope\",\"question\":\"For `Level 1`, which payload style should be mandatory for every domain agent?\",\"options\":[{\"label\":\"Rich typed envelope (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Include structured domain data plus context_used, citations, freshness, confidence, and errors in strict schema.\"},{\"label\":\"Domain data + citations only\",\"description\":\"Keep L1 lighter; context/failure details stay mostly in logs.\"},{\"label\":\"Text with labels\",\"description\":\"Use semi-structured text sections instead of strict typed fields.\"}]},{\"header\":\"L2 shape\",\"id\":\"level2_shape\",\"question\":\"For `Level 2`, what should backend return as the primary user-facing contract?\",\"options\":[{\"label\":\"Narrative + structured blocks (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Provide concise user text plus structured `risk_card`/`artifact` blocks for deterministic UI rendering.\"},{\"label\":\"Structured blocks only\",\"description\":\"Return only machine-readable objects; frontend generates all user text.\"},{\"label\":\"Narrative only\",\"description\":\"Return just formatted text with citations; no structured UI contract.\"}]},{\"header\":\"Consistency\",\"id\":\"consistency_strategy\",\"question\":\"How should we resolve mismatch between architecture and implementation spec?\",\"options\":[{\"label\":\"Architecture wins (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Make implementation spec conform fully to L1/L2 typed contract and deterministic merge.\"},{\"label\":\"Middle ground\",\"description\":\"Keep both styles documented as alternatives during transition.\"},{\"label\":\"Implementation wins\",\"description\":\"Retain current structured-text implementation as primary and loosen architecture language.\"}]}]}",
    "call_id": "call_gzpUJPPZSgqGNjnRgjbs0b2Z"
  }
}
```

## Entry 90
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:31:11.548Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:31:11.548Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cdc34-c8e6-7c01-95f0-9429ce05345c",
    "turn_id": "019cdc39-fbee-79e2-80bf-1e3aa0783667",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cdc39-fbee-79e2-80bf-1e3aa0783667-plan",
      "text": "## Level-1 / Level-2 Contract Alignment for MetroSense\n\n### Summary\nDefine and document a strict two-level output model where:\n- `Level 1` is a rich typed internal payload per domain agent (detailed context + evidence).\n- `Level 2` is the single user-facing backend contract (concise narrative + optional structured blocks).\n- `docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md` is updated to match `UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md` as the source direction.\n- Structured UI blocks (`risk_card`, `artifact`) are nullable until implemented, and only populated when explicitly requested and supported.\n\n### Key Contract Changes\n- `Level 1` mandatory envelope for every domain response:\n  - Required fields: `agent`, `query_id`, `timestamp`, `status`, `confidence`, `data`, `citations`, `data_freshness`, `errors`, `context_used`.\n  - `context_used` must capture reasoning inputs (resolved location, time window, domain intent, key thresholds/assumptions).\n  - `citations` must be typed (`database` or `document`) and claim-traceable.\n  - `status` enum fixed: `ok | partial | error`; `errors` required for non-`ok`.\n- `Level 2` single backend->frontend contract:\n  - Required: `response_text` (precise user-facing), `response_mode`, `citations_summary`, `data_freshness_summary`.\n  - Optional nullable blocks: `risk_card`, `artifact`, `follow_up_prompt`.\n  - Rule: `risk_card`/`artifact` only set when user explicitly requests relevant visual/structured output and feature is available; otherwise `null`.\n- Deterministic mapping layer:\n  - Add explicit “L1 -> L2 shaping rules” section in spec.\n  - Keep L1 detailed but avoid leaking raw internal/tool/debug fields directly into `response_text`.\n  - If any domain L1 is `partial/error`, L2 must degrade gracefully with explicit uncertainty text and preserved freshness/citation transparency.\n\n### Documentation Updates\n- Update [UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md):\n  - Clarify that L1 is internal contract and L2 is user delivery contract.\n  - Add concise “what users see vs what agents exchange” table.\n  - Add phased note: structured blocks nullable until implemented.\n- Update [docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md):\n  - Replace “Level-1 structured text with labelled sections” with strict typed L1 schema language.\n  - Add canonical L2 response schema and mapping rules.\n  - Align session flow wording to return L2 contract (not raw agent text extraction).\n- Keep [docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md) in sync:\n  - Frontend consumes one stable L2 shape.\n  - UI renders `response_text` always; renders `risk_card`/`artifact` only when non-null.\n\n### Test Plan\n- Spec consistency checks:\n  - Verify same field names/enums across both docs for L1 and L2.\n  - Verify no residual wording that L1 is text-formatted output.\n- Contract behavior scenarios:\n  - Normal domain answer: L2 contains `response_text`, summaries, null structured blocks.\n  - Explicit chart/graph request when unsupported: L2 keeps blocks null and returns precise capability message.\n  - Partial domain failure: L2 includes uncertainty statement and still includes available citations/freshness.\n  - Scorecard/structured request when implemented later: same L2 schema, non-null `risk_card`/`artifact`.\n- Acceptance criteria:\n  - One canonical L2 shape documented.\n  - L1 and L2 responsibilities are non-overlapping and explicit.\n  - No implementation decision left ambiguous for future coding.\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Architecture direction is authoritative when conflicts exist.\n- Current product state: risk cards/artifacts not yet implemented end-to-end.\n- Default behavior now: L2 returns narrative-first with nullable structured blocks.\n- Structured blocks are populated only by explicit user intent and feature readiness.\n"
    }
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**

renders `risk_card`/`artifact` only when non-null.\n\n### Test Plan\n- Spec consistency checks:\n  - Verify same field names/enums across both docs for L1 and L2.\n  - Verify no residual wording that L1 is text-formatted output.\n- Contract behavior scenarios:\n  - Normal domain answer: L2 contains `response_text`, summaries, null structured blocks.\n  - Explicit chart/graph request when unsupported: L2 keeps blocks null and returns precise capability message.\n  - Partial domain failure: L2 includes uncertainty statement and still includes available citations/freshness.\n  - Scorecard/structured request when implemented later: same L2 schema, non-null `risk_card`/`artifact`.\n- Acceptance criteria:\n  - One canonical L2 shape documented.\n  - L1 and L2 responsibilities are non-overlapping and explicit.\n  - No implementation decision left ambiguous for future coding.\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Architecture direction is authoritative when conflicts exist.\n- Current product state: risk cards/artifacts not yet implemented end-to-end.\n- Default behavior now: L2 returns narrative-first with nullable structured blocks.\n- Structured blocks are populated only by explicit user intent and feature readiness.\n"
    }
  }
}

        "text": "<proposed_plan>\n## Level-1 / Level-2 Contract Alignment for MetroSense\n\n### Summary\nDefine and document a strict two-level output model where:\n- `Level 1` is a rich typed internal payload per domain agent (detailed context + evidence).\n- `Level 2` is the single user-facing backend contract (concise narrative + optional structured blocks).\n- `docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md` is updated to match `UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md` as the source direction.\n- Structured UI blocks (`risk_card`, `artifact`) are nullable until implemented, and only populated when explicitly requested and supported.\n\n### Key Contract Changes\n- `Level 1` mandatory envelope for every domain response:\n  - Required fields: `agent`, `query_id`, `timestamp`, `status`, `confidence`, `data`, `citations`, `data_freshness`, `errors`, `context_used`.\n  - `context_used` must capture reasoning inputs (resolved location, time window, domain intent, key thresholds/assumptions).\n  - `citations` must be typed (`database` or `document`) and claim-traceable.\n  - `status` enum fixed: `ok | partial | error`; `errors` required for non-`ok`.\n- `Level 2` single backend->frontend contract:\n  - Required: `response_text` (precise user-facing), `response_mode`, `citations_summary`, `data_freshness_summary`.\n  - Optional nullable blocks: `risk_card`, `artifact`, `follow_up_prompt`.\n  - Rule: `risk_card`/`artifact` only set when user explicitly requests relevant visual/structured output and feature is available; otherwise `null`.\n- Deterministic mapping layer:\n  - Add explicit “L1 -> L2 shaping rules” section in spec.\n  - Keep L1 detailed but avoid leaking raw internal/tool/debug fields directly into `response_text`.\n  - If any domain L1 is `partial/error`, L2 must degrade gracefully with explicit uncertainty text and preserved freshness/citation transparency.\n\n### Documentation Updates\n- Update [UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md):\n  - Clarify that L1 is internal contract and L2 is user delivery contract.\n  - Add concise “what users see vs what agents exchange” table.\n  - Add phased note: structured blocks nullable until implemented.\n- Update [docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md):\n  - Replace “Level-1 structured text with labelled sections” with strict typed L1 schema language.\n  - Add canonical L2 response schema and mapping rules.\n  - Align session flow wording to return L2 contract (not raw agent text extraction).\n- Keep [docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md) in sync:\n  - Frontend consumes one stable L2 shape.\n  - UI renders `response_text` always; renders `risk_card`/`artifact` only when non-null.\n\n### Test Plan\n- Spec consistency checks:\n  - Verify same field names/enums across both docs for L1 and L2.\n  - Verify no residual wording that L1 is text-formatted output.\n- Contract behavior scenarios:\n  - Normal domain answer: L2 contains `response_text`, summaries, null structured blocks.\n  - Explicit chart/graph request when unsupported: L2 keeps blocks null and returns precise capability message.\n  - Partial domain failure: L2 includes uncertainty statement and still includes available citations/freshness.\n  - Scorecard/structured request when implemented later: same L2 schema, non-null `risk_card`/`artifact`.\n- Acceptance criteria:\n  - One canonical L2 shape documented.\n  - L1 and L2 responsibilities are non-overlapping and explicit.\n  - No implementation decision left ambiguous for future coding.\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Architecture direction is authoritative when conflicts exist.\n- Current product state: risk cards/artifacts not yet implemented end-to-end.\n- Default behavior now: L2 returns narrative-first with nullable structured blocks.\n- Structured blocks are populated only by explicit user intent and feature readiness.\n</proposed_plan>"

        "type": "output_text",
        "text": "<proposed_plan>\n## Level-1 / Level-2 Contract Alignment for MetroSense\n\n### Summary\nDefine and document a strict two-level output model where:\n- `Level 1` is a rich typed internal payload per domain agent (detailed context + evidence).\n- `Level 2` is the single user-facing backend contract (concise narrative + optional structured blocks).\n- `docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md` is updated to match `UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md` as the source direction.\n- Structured UI blocks (`risk_card`, `artifact`) are nullable until implemented, and only populated when explicitly requested and supported.\n\n### Key Contract Changes\n- `Level 1` mandatory envelope for every domain response:\n  - Required fields: `agent`, `query_id`, `timestamp`, `status`, `confidence`, `data`, `citations`, `data_freshness`, `errors`, `context_used`.\n  - `context_used` must capture reasoning inputs (resolved location, time window, domain intent, key thresholds/assumptions).\n  - `citations` must be typed (`database` or `document`) and claim-traceable.\n  - `status` enum fixed: `ok | partial | error`; `errors` required for non-`ok`.\n- `Level 2` single backend->frontend contract:\n  - Required: `response_text` (precise user-facing), `response_mode`, `citations_summary`, `data_freshness_summary`.\n  - Optional nullable blocks: `risk_card`, `artifact`, `follow_up_prompt`.\n  - Rule: `risk_card`/`artifact` only set when user explicitly requests relevant visual/structured output and feature is available; otherwise `null`.\n- Deterministic mapping layer:\n  - Add explicit “L1 -> L2 shaping rules” section in spec.\n  - Keep L1 detailed but avoid leaking raw internal/tool/debug fields directly into `response_text`.\n  - If any domain L1 is `partial/error`, L2 must degrade gracefully with explicit uncertainty text and preserved freshness/citation transparency.\n\n### Documentation Updates\n- Update [UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md):\n  - Clarify that L1 is internal contract and L2 is user delivery contract.\n  - Add concise “what users see vs what agents exchange” table.\n  - Add phased note: structured blocks nullable until implemented.\n- Update [docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md):\n  - Replace “Level-1 structured text with labelled sections” with strict typed L1 schema language.\n  - Add canonical L2 response schema and mapping rules.\n  - Align session flow wording to return L2 contract (not raw agent text extraction).\n- Keep [docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md) in sync:\n  - Frontend consumes one stable L2 shape.\n  - UI renders `response_text` always; renders `risk_card`/`artifact` only when non-null.\n\n### Test Plan\n- Spec consistency checks:\n  - Verify same field names/enums across both docs for L1 and L2.\n  - Verify no residual wording that L1 is text-formatted output.\n- Contract behavior scenarios:\n  - Normal domain answer: L2 contains `response_text`, summaries, null structured blocks.\n  - Explicit chart/graph request when unsupported: L2 keeps blocks null and returns precise capability message.\n  - Partial domain failure: L2 includes uncertainty statement and still includes available citations/freshness.\n  - Scorecard/structured request when implemented later: same L2 schema, non-null `risk_card`/`artifact`.\n- Acceptance criteria:\n  - One canonical L2 shape documented.\n  - L1 and L2 responsibilities are non-overlapping and explicit.\n  - No implementation decision left ambiguous for future coding.\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Architecture direction is authoritative when conflicts exist.\n- Current product state: risk cards/artifacts not yet implemented end-to-end.\n- Default behavior now: L2 returns narrative-first with nullable structured blocks.\n- Structured blocks are populated only by explicit user intent and feature readiness.\n</proposed_plan>"
      }
    ],
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

## Entry 96
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:31:12.526Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:31:12.526Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc3c-690c-7970-8354-f25d260724a6",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

No LLM call is involved in this merge step — it is deterministic.\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:66:Domain Agent Output  →  Pydantic Level-1 Payload  (per domain)\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:67:Orchestrator Merge   →  Pydantic Level-2 Scorecard (unified)\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:119:Every agent response includes a `data_freshness` block telling the user when the underlying data was last updated. Never talks to the user directly. This makes hallucination checking deterministic.\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:473:  synthesize_nl_response(results[])         -- final LLM call with citations\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:498:### Level-1 — Domain Payload Envelope (every agent returns this shape)\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:508:  \"citations\": [\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:522:  \"data_freshness\": {\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:531:### Level-2 — Unified Risk Scorecard (Orchestrator pure Python merge)\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:588:  \"data_freshness\": {\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:627:          Combined into one answer with citations\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:936:data_freshness_lag_seconds\nUrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md:1276:Option A — Citation coverage (always on, built into guardrails): Every claim in the NL output must have a citation referencing a DB record ID or document section ID. |\n|---|---|\n| UI Button | User clicks → prompted for ward/location → full scorecard generated |\n| NL keyword | \"risk\", \"scorecard\", \"vulnerability\", \"assessment\", \"how vulnerable is [place]\" |\n| Explicitly excluded | \"summary\" — too generic, reserved for other conversational uses |\n\n---\n\n## 7. Data Architecture Overview\n\n### Two Sources, One Answer\n\n```\nUser Query\n    │\n    ├──────────────────────────────────────┐\n    │                                      │\n    ▼                                      ▼\nPostgreSQL                          Agentic RAG\nStructured sensor + telemetry       JSON-indexed documents\n    │                                      │\nAnswers numbers questions:          Answers document questions:\n\"AQI in Hebbal right now?\"          \"What does the drainage plan\n"
  }
}

## Entry 124
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:31:43.293Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:31:43.293Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_fGC5vd0D55OdZv96Pj3B1tgf",
    "output": "Chunk ID: eee69c\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 356\nOutput:\n│  • Returns complete structured response — NL text + scorecard JSON   │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n### Communication Pattern\n\n**Frontend → Backend:**\n```json\nPOST /chat\nRequest:\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n  \"message\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight which underpasses need barricading?\",\n  \"user_role\": \"bbmp_internal\"\n}\n\nResponse:\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n  \"response_text\": \"Based on current conditions...\",\n  \"scorecard\": null,\n  \"chart_followup\": null,\n  \"citations\": [],\n  \"data_freshness\": {}\n}\n```\n\n**Backend → ADK Server (internal):**\n```json\nPOST /agent/run\nRequest:\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n  \"message\": \"...\",\n  \"history\": [],\n  \"user_role\": \"bbmp_internal\"\n}\n\nResponse: same shape as above\n```\n\n---\n\n### Data Access — Development vs Production\n\n**Development (v1):** Domain agents connect directly to Postgres and the document file system. Fewer hops, faster to build, easier to debug locally.\n\n```\nDomain Agent → direct connection → Postgres\nDomain Agent → direct file access → /documents/indexes/ and /documents/sections/\n```\n\n**Production (future):** Data access moves behind Backend APIs. ADK server becomes fully stateless. Proper security boundaries enforced without changing agent logic.\n\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 125
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:31:43.296Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:31:43.296Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_XZty8GggFA1Iy3lzlw83vEJF",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 022735\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 645\nOutput:\nasync def get_traffic_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 5) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict]:\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Prompts (`prompts.py`)\n\nAll instruction strings live here. Never inline strings in agent files. Structure:\n\n```python\nROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nCHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nFLOOD_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nHEAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nINFRA_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nLOGISTICS_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\n```\n\nEach instruction includes:\n- Role and domain scope\n- Explicit list of tools available (agents reason better when told what they can call)\n- Output format for Level-1 domain payload (structured text with labelled sections)\n- Citation requirements\n- Data freshness: include `observed_at` of the most recent record used\n\n### Guardrail strings embedded in CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION\n\n```\nOFF_DOMAIN_REFUSAL = \"I can only answer questions about Bengaluru's climate, flood risk, \nair quality, power grid, and traffic. Please rephrase your question.\"\n\nLOCATION_NOT_FOUND = \"I couldn't find '{name}' in the Bengaluru location registry. \nPlease use a ward name or neighbourhood (e.g. 'Bellandur', 'Koramangala', 'Whitefield').\"\n\nNO_DATA_AVAILABLE = \"No recent data is available for {location} on {domain}. \nThe dataset covers historical records — please check the data freshness.\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Session Persistence Flow\n\n```\nUser → POST /api/chat\n    │\n    ▼\nchat route (FastAPI)\n    │ ─── calls ──→ agent_proxy.get_chat_response(settings, db_session, session_id, message)\n    │                   │\n    │                   ├─ 1. _ensure_session (ADK InMemory)\n    │                   ├─ 2. POST /run → ADK → root_agent → chat_agent → domain agents\n    │                   ├─ 3. extract message_text from events\n    │                   ├─ 4. conversation_service.upsert_session(db_session, session_id)\n    │                   ├─ 5. conversation_service.append_turn(db_session, session_id, ...)\n    │                   └─ 6. await db_session.commit()\n    │\n    ▼\nChatResponse returned to user\n```\n\nThe DB write happens **after** the ADK call succeeds. If the DB write fails, the response is still returned to the user (log the error, don't fail the request). This is a best-effort persistence approach — data availability > perfect persistence.\n\n---\n\n## 9. Environment Variables\n\n### Backend additions (`server/.env`)\n\nNo new variables needed — the backend already has `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN`. The internal routes use it for verification.\n\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 126
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:31:43.300Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:31:43.300Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_tJjRqr8dMpzBARiqR7lSJH97",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 6125be\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 531\nOutput:\n### Phase 5 — Docker + integration\n\n26. Update `docker-compose.yml` (volume + env vars)\n27. Update `agents/.env.example`\n28. Run full smoke test through backend → proxy → ADK → domain agents → DB persistence\n\n---\n\n## 13. Testing Checkpoints\n\n| Checkpoint | What to verify |\n|------------|----------------|\n| Phase 1 done | All 11 internal endpoints return data for known location_ids from the golden dataset |\n| Phase 2 done | `sessions` + `conversation_history` rows written after each `/api/chat` call |\n| Phase 3 done | Each tool function returns non-empty list for a known location_id |\n| Phase 4 done | `adk web` can complete a flood query, a health query, and a scorecard query |\n| Phase 5 done | Full Docker stack end-to-end: frontend chat → backend → proxy → ADK → domain agents → DB |\n\n---\n\n## 14. What Is Out of Scope for This Phase\n\n- Streaming / SSE (confirmed not in scope)\n- AuditLog writes (schema exists, writes added in a later phase after agent invocation tracking is wired)\n- Chart follow-up prompts (agent instruction includes the hook, UI not yet wired)\n- Scorecard rendering in the frontend (agent returns structured text; UI RiskCard integration is separate)\n- Sub-agent splitting (current agent files stay flat; sub-agents introduced when any agent > 300 lines)\n- google_search tool removal (metrosearch_agent still exists and works; new agent replaces it for new sessions)\n\n---\n\n## 15. Known Risks\n\n| Risk | Mitigation |\n|------|------------|\n| ADK sub_agent transfer reliability | Test each routing path explicitly in Phase 4 before moving to Phase 5 |\n| `resolve_location` returning zero results | Chat agent instruction handles `LOCATION_NOT_FOUND` case and asks user to rephrase |\n| Documents volume not mounted in dev | `DOCUMENTS_PATH` defaults to a local path; document tools return empty list gracefully on `FileNotFoundError` |\n| DB write fails after ADK response | Best-effort persistence — log error, return response to user regardless |\n| `conversation_history` → `sessions` FK violation on first turn | `upsert_session` runs before `append_turn` in the flow |\n"
  }
}
```

{

    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md\n@@\n-**Decision:** JSON output is enforced as a **typed contract at every agent boundary** using Pydantic v2 strict mode. Every domain agent returns a Level-1 typed payload. The Orchestrator runs a pure Python merge function to produce the Level-2 Unified Risk Scorecard. No LLM call is involved in this merge step — it is deterministic.\n+**Decision:** JSON output is enforced as a **typed contract at every agent boundary** using Pydantic v2 strict mode. Every domain agent returns a Level-1 typed payload. The Orchestrator runs a pure Python merge function to produce Level-2 structured blocks (risk card/artifact) when requested and available. The Chat Agent then returns the canonical Level-2 user delivery contract for the frontend. No extra formatting agent is added.\n@@\n-Domain Agent Output  →  Pydantic Level-1 Payload  (per domain)\n-Orchestrator Merge   →  Pydantic Level-2 Scorecard (unified)\n+Domain Agent Output  →  Pydantic Level-1 Payload  (per domain)\n+Orchestrator Merge   →  Level-2 Structured Blocks  (deterministic)\n+Chat Agent Output    →  Level-2 User Delivery Contract (single frontend shape)\n@@\n ### Level-1 — Domain Payload Envelope (every agent returns this shape)\n@@\n   \"status\": \"ok | partial | error\",\n   \"data\": {},\n+  \"context_used\": {\n+    \"resolved_location_id\": \"loc_bellandur_001\",\n+    \"resolved_location_name\": \"Bellandur\",\n+    \"intent\": \"flood_risk\",\n+    \"time_window\": \"next_6h\",\n+    \"assumptions\": [\n+      \"Rainfall threshold sourced from drainage_plan_2019_07 section 4.2\"\n+    ]\n+  },\n   \"citations\": [\n@@\n   \"errors\": []\n }\n ```\n \n-### Level-2 — Unified Risk Scorecard (Orchestrator pure Python merge)\n+`context_used` is mandatory in Level-1 so downstream synthesis remains explainable and auditable.\n+\n+### Level-2 — User Delivery Contract (backend to frontend)\n \n ```json\n {\n-  \"scorecard_id\": \"uuid-abc\",\n-  \"generated_at\": \"2024-07-15T18:30:05+05:30\",\n-  \"query_context\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses need barricading?\",\n-  \"neighborhoods\": [\n-    {\n-      \"name\": \"Bellandur\",\n-      \"ward_id\": \"ward_042\",\n-      \"flood_probability\": 0.91,\n-      \"flood_risk_tier\": \"CRITICAL\",\n-      \"power_outage_risk\": 0.62,\n-      \"traffic_delay_index\": 2.4,\n-      \"heat_health_risk\": 0.22,\n-      \"treefall_risk\": 0.38,\n-      \"barricade_recommended\": true,\n-      \"barricade_locations\": [\n-        \"Outer Ring Road underpass km 14\",\n-        \"Agara underpass\"\n-      ],\n-      \"emergency_readiness\": {\n-        \"recommendation\": \"EVACUATE low-lying streets by 20:00\",\n-        \"resource_alert\": \"Pre-position NDRF unit at Bellandur Junction\",\n-        \"confidence\": 0.87\n-      }\n-    }\n-  ],\n-  \"key_drivers\": [\n-    {\n-      \"factor\": \"Rainfall intensity\",\n-      \"value\": \"62mm in 3h\",\n-      \"impact\": \"high\"\n-    },\n-    {\n-      \"factor\": \"Drainage capacity gap\",\n-      \"value\": \"Documented undersized culvert near ORR crossing\",\n-      \"impact\": \"high\",\n-      \"citation\": {\n-        \"document_id\": \"drainage_plan_2019_07\",\n-        \"page\": 84\n-      }\n-    },\n-    {\n-      \"factor\": \"Lake backflow risk\",\n-      \"value\": \"Bellandur fill 92%\",\n-      \"impact\": \"medium\"\n-    }\n-  ],\n-  \"global_summary\": {\n-    \"total_zones_analyzed\": 12,\n-    \"critical_zones\": 2,\n-    \"high_risk_zones\": 4,\n-    \"logistics_fleet_advisory\": \"Expect 2.1x average TTD delay on ORR corridor\",\n-    \"overall_emergency_posture\": \"ELEVATED\"\n-  },\n-  \"data_freshness\": {\n-    \"telemetry_lag_seconds\": 42,\n-    \"last_imd_bulletin\": \"2024-07-15T06:00:00+05:30\",\n-    \"pg_query_time_ms\": 310\n-  }\n+  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n+  \"response_mode\": \"text | scorecard | artifact\",\n+  \"response_text\": \"Bellandur flood risk is high for tonight due to rainfall intensity and lake fill levels.\",\n+  \"citations_summary\": [\n+    {\n+      \"source_type\": \"database\",\n+      \"source_id\": \"lake_hydrology:lake_003\",\n+      \"label\": \"Bellandur fill percentage\"\n+    },\n+    {\n+      \"source_type\": \"document\",\n+      \"source_id\": \"drainage_plan_2019_07#section_4_2\",\n+      \"label\": \"ORR culvert capacity\"\n+    }\n+  ],\n+  \"data_freshness_summary\": {\n+    \"telemetry_lag_seconds\": 42,\n+    \"stale_sources\": []\n+  },\n+  \"risk_card\": null,\n+  \"artifact\": null,\n+  \"follow_up_prompt\": \"Would you like this as a chart?\"\n }\n ```\n+\n+`risk_card` and `artifact` are nullable and populated only when explicitly requested by the user and supported by implemented capabilities.\n+\n+### Level-2 Structured Block Example — `risk_card`\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"risk_card\": {\n+    \"scorecard_id\": ## Entry 140
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:32:55.530Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:32:55.530Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_pAkanEiLgdiGr9duKYtsdyY6",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/docs/AGENT_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC.md\n@@\n Each instruction includes:\n - Role and domain scope\n - Explicit list of tools available (agents reason better when told what they can call)\n-- Output format for Level-1 domain payload (structured text with labelled sections)\n+- Output format for Level-1 domain payload (strict typed JSON envelope)\n - Citation requirements\n - Data freshness: include `observed_at` of the most recent record used\n+- Context requirements: include resolved location, time window, and key assumptions in `context_used`\n@@\n ## 8. Session Persistence Flow\n@@\n-    │                   ├─ 3. extract message_text from events\n+    │                   ├─ 3. map ADK output to canonical Level-2 response contract\n     │                   ├─ 4. conversation_service.upsert_session(db_session, session_id)\n     │                   ├─ 5. conversation_service.append_turn(db_session, session_id, ...)\n     │                   └─ 6. await db_session.commit()\n     │\n     ▼\n-ChatResponse returned to user\n+Level-2 response returned to user\n ```\n@@\n The DB write happens **after** the ADK call succeeds. If the DB write fails, the response is still returned to the user (log the error, don't fail the request). This is a best-effort persistence approach — data availability > perfect persistence.\n+\n+### 8.1 Canonical response contracts (Level-1 vs Level-2)\n+\n+Level-1 is internal and mandatory for every domain agent call. Level-2 is the only backend response shape exposed to frontend clients.\n+\n+**Level-1 domain payload (internal)**\n+- Strict typed schema with required fields: `agent`, `query_id`, `timestamp`, `status`, `confidence`, `data`, `citations`, `data_freshness`, `context_used`, `errors`\n+- `status` enum is `ok | partial | error`\n+- `context_used` is required to capture resolved location, intent, and operational assumptions\n+- L1 payloads are not rendered directly in UI\n+\n+**Level-2 user delivery contract (backend -> frontend)**\n+- Single stable shape for all chat responses:\n+  - `session_id`\n+  - `response_mode` (`text | scorecard | artifact`)\n+  - `response_text` (always present)\n+  - `citations_summary` (always present, may be empty)\n+  - `data_freshness_summary` (always present)\n+  - `risk_card` (nullable)\n+  - `artifact` (nullable)\n+  - `follow_up_prompt` (nullable)\n+- `risk_card` and `artifact` are populated only when the user explicitly asks and the capability is implemented; otherwise they remain `null`\n+- Mapping from Level-1 -> Level-2 is deterministic (no separate formatting agent)\n+\n+Example Level-2 response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n+  \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n+  \"response_text\": \"Bellandur flood risk is elevated tonight due to rainfall and lake fill conditions.\",\n+  \"citations_summary\": [],\n+  \"data_freshness_summary\": {\n+    \"telemetry_lag_seconds\": 42,\n+    \"stale_sources\": []\n+  },\n+  \"risk_card\": null,\n+  \"artifact\": null,\n+  \"follow_up_prompt\": null\n+}\n+```\n@@\n - Streaming / SSE (confirmed not in scope)\n - AuditLog writes (schema exists, writes added in a later phase after agent invocation tracking is wired)\n - Chart follow-up prompts (agent instruction includes the hook, UI not yet wired)\n-- Scorecard rendering in the frontend (agent returns structured text; UI RiskCard integration is separate)\n+- Non-null `risk_card` / `artifact` rendering in the frontend (Level-2 fields exist now but are null until those capabilities are implemented)\n - Sub-agent splitting (current agent files stay flat; sub-agents introduced when any agent > 300 lines)\n - google_search tool removal (metrosearch_agent still exists and works; new agent replaces it for new sessions)\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json

If the DB write fails, the response is still returned to the user (log the error, don't fail the request). otherwise they remain `null`\n+- Mapping from Level-1 -> Level-2 is deterministic (no separate formatting agent)\n+\n+Example Level-2 response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n+  \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n+  \"response_text\": \"Bellandur flood risk is elevated tonight due to rainfall and lake fill conditions.\",\n+  \"citations_summary\": [],\n+  \"data_freshness_summary\": {\n+    \"telemetry_lag_seconds\": 42,\n+    \"stale_sources\": []\n+  },\n+  \"risk_card\": null,\n+  \"artifact\": null,\n+  \"follow_up_prompt\": null\n+}\n+```\n@@\n - Streaming / SSE (confirmed not in scope)\n - AuditLog writes (schema exists, writes added in a later phase after agent invocation tracking is wired)\n - Chart follow-up prompts (agent instruction includes the hook, UI not yet wired)\n-- Scorecard rendering in the frontend (agent returns structured text; UI RiskCard integration is separate)\n+- Non-null `risk_card` / `artifact` rendering in the frontend (Level-2 fields exist now but are null until those capabilities are implemented)\n - Sub-agent splitting (current agent files stay flat; sub-agents introduced when any agent > 300 lines)\n - google_search tool removal (metrosearch_agent still exists and works; new agent replaces it for new sessions)\n*** End Patch\n"

**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:33:34.193Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_YYAl86QTnkWt5iOWR391bO8K",
    "output": "Chunk ID: d62e29\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2759\nOutput:\ndiff --git a/UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md b/UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md\nindex 0ec90c1..641cdd2 100644\n--- a/UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md\n+++ b/UrbanClimate_AI_Architecture.md\n@@ -60,11 +60,12 @@ UrbanClimate AI is a **multi-agent orchestration system** built on Google ADK. I\n \n **Problem:** Adds a redundant LLM call, introduces a fragile parsing step, and creates an unnecessary agent boundary.\n \n-**Decision:** JSON output is enforced as a **typed contract at every agent boundary** using Pydantic v2 strict mode. Every domain agent returns a Level-1 typed payload. The Orchestrator runs a pure Python merge function to produce the Level-2 Unified Risk Scorecard. No LLM call is involved in this merge step — it is deterministic.\n+**Decision:** JSON output is enforced as a **typed contract at every agent boundary** using Pydantic v2 strict mode. Every domain agent returns a Level-1 typed payload. The Orchestrator runs a pure Python merge function to produce Level-2 structured blocks (risk card/artifact) when requested and available. The Chat Agent then returns the canonical Level-2 user delivery contract for the frontend. No extra formatting agent is added.\n \n ```\n Domain Agent Output  →  Pydantic Level-1 Payload  (per domain)\n-Orchestrator Merge   →  Pydantic Level-2 Scorecard (unified)\n+Orchestrator Merge   →  Level-2 Structured Blocks  (deterministic)\n+Chat Agent Output    →  Level-2 User Delivery Contract (single frontend shape)\n ```\n \n ---\n@@ -160,7 +161,7 @@ Chat Agent (main conversational interface — only agent user talks to)\n \n **Runner:** The ADK Runner is not an agent you design. It is the ADK infrastructure that executes the Root Agent, manages the event loop, and handles tool call cycles. You configure it in one line — `Runner(agent=root_agent)`. It is not a component in the architecture diagram.\n \n-**Root Agent:** Pure routing and state management. Never talks to the user directly. Owns parallel dispatch, global guardrails, and the Level-1 to Level-2 scorecard merge.\n+**Root Agent:** Pure routing and state management. Never talks to the user directly. Owns parallel dispatch, global guardrails, and deterministic Level-1 to Level-2 structured block merge.\n \n **Chat Agent:** The only agent that has a conversation with the user. Owns session memory, NL synthesis, citation formatting, chart follow-up prompts, and scorecard triggers. It calls domain agents directly — no round-trip back through the Root Agent.\n \n@@ -197,7 +198,7 @@ The folder structure pre-partitions tool logic by concern inside each agent so s\n │                                                                      │\n │  • Route intent, manage state bag                                    │\n │  • Parallel dispatch via asyncio.gather()                            │\n-│  • Merge Level-1 payloads → Level-2 Scorecard                       │\n+│  • Merge Level-1 payloads → Level-2 structured blocks               │\n │  • Apply global guardrails                                           │\n └───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                             │\n@@ -225,7 +226,7 @@ AGENT          AGENT         AGENT          AGENT\n                     Each returns Level-1 Pydantic Payload\n                                │\n                     Chat Agent synthesizes NL response\n-                    Root Agent merges → Level-2 Scorecard\n+                    Root Agent merges → Level-2 structured blocks\n ```\n \n ---\n@@ -264,8 +265,8 @@ Flood Agent                      Logistics Agent\n         └──────────────┬───────────────────┘\n                        ▼\n Chat Agent collects Level-1 payloads\n-Root Agent merges → Level-2 Unified Scorecard\n-Chat Agent synthesizes NL answer with citations\n+Root Agent merges → Level-2 structured blocks (risk_card/artifact when applicable)\n+Chat Agent synthesizes canonical Level-2 user response with citations\n         │\n         │ Complete response JSON\n         ▼\n@@ -480,7 +481,7 @@ Document RAG Tools:\n \"If 60mm tonight, which underpasses?\"      → Flood + Logistics (parallel)\n \"Compare humidity vs decade average\"       → Heat Agent (historical mode)\n \"Tree-fall risk near MG Road?\"             → Infra Agent (zone filter)\n-\"Risk scorecard for Bellandur\"             → All agents → Unified Scorecard\n+\"Risk scorecard for Bellandur\"             → All agents → structured `risk_card` block\n \"Will ORR delay cross 1.5x tonight?\"       → Logistics Agent only\n ```\n \n@@ -505,6 +506,15 @@ After any trend or comparative answer, Chat Agent evaluates whether a chart woul\n   \"confidence\": 0.87,\n   \"status\": \"ok | partial | error\",\n   \"data\": {},\n+  \"context_used\": {\n+    \"resolved_location_id\": \"loc_bellandur_001\",\n+    \"resolved_location_name\": \"Bellandur\",\n+    \"intent\": \"flood_risk\",\n+    \"time_window\": \"next_6h\",\n+    \"assumptions\": [\n+      \"Rainfall threshold sourced from drainage_plan_2019_07 section 4.2\"\n+    ]\n+  },\n   \"citations\": [\n     {\n       \"type\": \"database\",\n@@ -528,67 +538,65 @@ After any trend or comparative answer, Chat Agent evaluates whether a chart woul\n }\n ```\n \n-### Level-2 — Unified Risk Scorecard (Orchestrator pure Python merge)\n+`context_used` is mandatory in Level-1 so downstream synthesis remains explainable and auditable.\n+\n+### Level-2 — User Delivery Contract (backend to frontend)\n \n ```json\n {\n-  \"scorecard_id\": \"uuid-abc\",\n-  \"generated_at\": \"2024-07-15T18:30:05+05:30\",\n-  \"query_context\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses need barricading?\",\n-  \"neighborhoods\": [\n-    {\n-      \"name\": \"Bellandur\",\n-      \"ward_id\": \"ward_042\",\n-      \"flood_probability\": 0.91,\n-      \"flood_risk_tier\": \"CRITICAL\",\n-      \"power_outage_risk\": 0.62,\n-      \"traffic_delay_index\": 2.4,\n-      \"heat_health_risk\": 0.22,\n-      \"treefall_risk\": 0.38,\n-      \"barricade_recommended\": true,\n-      \"barricade_locations\": [\n-        \"Outer Ring Road underpass km 14\",\n-        \"Agara underpass\"\n-      ],\n-      \"emergency_readiness\": {\n-        \"recommendation\": \"EVACUATE low-lying streets by 20:00\",\n-        \"resource_alert\": \"Pre-position NDRF unit at Bellandur Junction\",\n-        \"confidence\": 0.87\n-      }\n-    }\n-  ],\n-  \"key_drivers\": [\n-    {\n-      \"factor\": \"Rainfall intensity\",\n-      \"value\": \"62mm in 3h\",\n-      \"impact\": \"high\"\n-    },\n+  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n+  \"response_mode\": \"text | scorecard | artifact\",\n+  \"response_text\": \"Bellandur flood risk is high for tonight due to rainfall intensity and lake fill levels.\",\n+  \"citations_summary\": [\n     {\n-      \"factor\": \"Drainage capacity gap\",\n-      \"value\": \"Documented undersized culvert near ORR crossing\",\n-      \"impact\": \"high\",\n-      \"citation\": {\n-        \"document_id\": \"drainage_plan_2019_07\",\n-        \"page\": 84\n-      }\n+      \"source_type\": \"database\",\n+      \"source_id\": \"lake_hydrology:lake_003\",\n+      \"label\": \"Bellandur fill percentage\"\n     },\n     {\n-      \"factor\": \"Lake backflow risk\",\n-      \"value\": \"Bellandur fill 92%\",\n-      \"impact\": \"medium\"\n+      \"source_type\": \"document\",\n+      \"source_id\": \"drainage_plan_2019_07#section_4_2\",\n+      \"label\": \"ORR culvert capacity\"\n     }\n   ],\n-  \"global_summary\": {\n-    \"total_zones_analyzed\": 12,\n-    \"critical_zones\": 2,\n-    \"high_risk_zones\": 4,\n-    \"logistics_fleet_advisory\": \"Expect 2.1x average TTD delay on ORR corridor\",\n-    \"overall_emergency_posture\": \"ELEVATED\"\n-  },\n-  \"data_freshness\": {\n+  \"data_freshness_summary\": {\n     \"telemetry_lag_seconds\": 42,\n-    \"last_imd_bulletin\": \"2024-07-15T06:00:00+05:30\",\n-    \"pg_query_time_ms\": 310\n+    \"stale_sources\": []\n+  },\n+  \"risk_card\": null,\n+  \"artifact\": null,\n+  \"follow_up_prompt\": \"Would you like this as a chart?\"\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`risk_card` and `artifact` are nullable and populated only when explicitly requested by the user and supported by implemented capabilities.\n+\n+### Level-2 Structured Block Example — `risk_card`\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"risk_card\": {\n+    \"scorecard_id\": \"uuid-abc\",\n+    \"generated_at\": \"2024-07-15T18:30:05+05:30\",\n+    \"query_context\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses need barricading?\",\n+    \"neighborhoods\": [\n+      {\n+        \"name\": \"Bellandur\",\n+        \"ward_id\": \"ward_042\",\n+        \"flood_probability\": 0.91,\n+        \"flood_risk_tier\": \"CRITICAL\",\n+        \"power_outage_risk\": 0.62,\n+        \"traffic_delay_index\": 2.4,\n+        \"heat_health_risk\": 0.22,\n+        \"treefall_risk\": 0.38,\n+        \"barricade_recommended\": true\n+      }\n+    ],\n+    \"global_summary\": {\n+      \"total_zones_analyzed\": 12,\n+      \"critical_zones\": 2,\n+      \"high_risk_zones\": 4\n+    }\n   }\n }\n ```\n@@ -917,7 +925,7 @@ session_id\n role                   -- user / assistant\n message\n agents_invoked\n-response_mode          -- text / scorecard / chart\n+response_mode          -- text / scorecard / artifact\n cited_documents\n cited_records\n timestamp\n@@ -1357,7 +1365,7 @@ The system is split into three independently deployable layers. Each layer has a\n │  • ADK Runner executes Root Agent                                    │\n │  • Root Agent → Chat Agent → Domain Sub-Agents                      │\n │  • Domain agents connect directly to Postgres + file storage (dev)  │\n-│  • Returns complete structured response — NL text + scorecard JSON   │\n+│  • Returns complete structured response — NL text + nullable blocks  │\n └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n ```\n \n@@ -1375,14 +1383,16 @@ Request:\n   \"user_role\": \"bbmp_internal\"\n }\n \n-Response:\n+Response (canonical Level-2 shape):\n {\n   \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n+  \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n   \"response_text\": \"Based on current conditions...\",\n-  \"scorecard\": null,\n-  \"chart_followup\": null,\n-  \"citations\": [],\n-  \"data_freshness\": {}\n+  \"citations_summary\": [],\n+  \"data_freshness_summary\": {},\n+  \"risk_card\": null,\n+  \"artifact\": null,\n+  \"follow_up_prompt\": null\n }\n ```\n \n@@ -1397,7 +1407,7 @@ Request:\n   \"user_role\": \"bbmp_internal\"\n }\n \n-Response: same shape as above\n+Response: same canonical Level-2 shape as above\n ```\n \n ---\n@@ -1467,7 +1477,7 @@ urbanclimate_ai/\n │   │   ├── agent.py                     # Root OrchestratorAgent\n │   │   ├── intent_classifier.py         # Maps NL query to domain intents\n │   │   ├── context_manager.py           # Shared session state bag\n-│   │   └── scorecard_merger.py          # Pure Python Level-1 to Level-2 merge\n+│   │   └── scorecard_merger.py          # Pure Python Level-1 to Level-2 structured merge\n │   │\n │   ├── agents/\n │   │   ├── shared/\n"
  }
}
