Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 285
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:09:06.421Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:09:06.421Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cdc34-c8e6-7c01-95f0-9429ce05345c",
    "turn_id": "019cdc5e-d3df-7352-aca6-7f726a752706",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cdc5e-d3df-7352-aca6-7f726a752706-plan",
      "text": "## Phase 3 Rewrite: Tools Implementation With Proper Agents File Structure\n\n### Summary\nImplement Phase 3 by creating a production-ready `metrosense_agent` package layout and completing all data/document tools.  \nTools will serve one purpose: provide reliable context/data to agents from backend APIs and local filesystem documents, with the same behavior in local and Docker via env configuration.\n\n### Implementation Changes\n- Establish canonical package structure under `agents/metrosense_agent/`:\n  - `subagents/` for agent logic\n  - `tools/` for all data access\n  - `tools/shared/` for cross-domain helpers\n  - `tools/types.py` for shared tool result contract/error codes\n  - `tests/` for tool-level tests\n- Implement backend tool stack:\n  - `tools/backend_client.py` with token-authenticated async HTTP client for `/internal/*`\n  - Domain/shared modules (`weather_tools.py`, `location_tools.py`, `flood_tools.py`, `heat_tools.py`, `infra_tools.py`, `logistics_tools.py`) mapped to live internal endpoints\n- Implement document tool stack:\n  - `tools/shared/document_tools.py` reads only from `DOCUMENTS_PATH` (strict env mode)\n  - Support both local and Docker by setting `DOCUMENTS_PATH` appropriately\n  - Add filters in `list_document_indexes`: `document_type`, `ward_name`, `contains_thresholds`\n- Standardize all tool outputs:\n  - Every tool returns `{data, meta}`\n  - `meta` includes `ok`, `source`, `error_code`, `error_detail`\n  - Failures return empty data and `meta.ok=false` (no hard crash)\n\n### Public Interfaces / Types\n- Shared tool return envelope (single contract for all tools):\n  - `data: list | dict | null`\n  - `meta.ok: bool`\n  - `meta.source: \"backend\" | \"filesystem\"`\n  - `meta.error_code: str | null`\n  - `meta.error_detail: str | null`\n- Required env vars for agents:\n  - `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL`\n  - `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN`\n  - `DOCUMENTS_PATH`\n- Documented error codes (minimum):\n  - `BACKEND_TIMEOUT`, `BACKEND_HTTP_ERROR`, `BACKEND_UNAUTHORIZED`\n  - `DOCUMENTS_PATH_MISSING`, `INDEX_PARSE_ERROR`, `SECTION_NOT_FOUND`, `FILE_READ_ERROR`\n\n### Test Cases and Scenarios\n- Backend client tests:\n  - success, timeout, non-2xx, unauthorized token\n- Document tools tests:\n  - valid index load, filter behavior, missing section, malformed index, missing path\n- Domain tool integration smoke:\n  - one success call per module against known IDs\n  - forced failure verifies graceful envelope response\n- Acceptance checks:\n  - all Step 3 modules import cleanly\n  - every tool follows `{data, meta}` contract\n  - local + Docker behavior differs only by env values, not code path\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Phase 1 and Phase 2 are already complete and internal endpoints are available.\n- `DOCUMENTS_PATH` is mandatory (no auto-fallback path search).\n- Phase 4 subagents will consume tool `meta` to adjust confidence and user phrasing during degraded data states.\n"
    }
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json

      "text": "## Phase 3 Rewrite: Tools Implementation With Proper Agents File Structure\n\n### Summary\nImplement Phase 3 by creating a production-ready `metrosense_agent` package layout and completing all data/document tools.  \nTools will serve one purpose: provide reliable context/data to agents from backend APIs and local filesystem documents, with the same behavior in local and Docker via env configuration.\n\n### Implementation Changes\n- Establish canonical package structure under `agents/metrosense_agent/`:\n  - `subagents/` for agent logic\n  - `tools/` for all data access\n  - `tools/shared/` for cross-domain helpers\n  - `tools/types.py` for shared tool result contract/error codes\n  - `tests/` for tool-level tests\n- Implement backend tool stack:\n  - `tools/backend_client.py` with token-authenticated async HTTP client for `/internal/*`\n  - Domain/shared modules (`weather_tools.py`, `location_tools.py`, `flood_tools.py`, `heat_tools.py`, `infra_tools.py`, `logistics_tools.py`) mapped to live internal endpoints\n- Implement document tool stack:\n  - `tools/shared/document_tools.py` reads only from `DOCUMENTS_PATH` (strict env mode)\n  - Support both local and Docker by setting `DOCUMENTS_PATH` appropriately\n  - Add filters in `list_document_indexes`: `document_type`, `ward_name`, `contains_thresholds`\n- Standardize all tool outputs:\n  - Every tool returns `{data, meta}`\n  - `meta` includes `ok`, `source`, `error_code`, `error_detail`\n  - Failures return empty data and `meta.ok=false` (no hard crash)\n\n### Public Interfaces / Types\n- Shared tool return envelope (single contract for all tools):\n  - `data: list | dict | null`\n  - `meta.ok: bool`\n  - `meta.source: \"backend\" | \"filesystem\"`\n  - `meta.error_code: str | null`\n  - `meta.error_detail: str | null`\n- Required env vars for agents:\n  - `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL`\n  - `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN`\n  - `DOCUMENTS_PATH`\n- Documented error codes (minimum):\n  - `BACKEND_TIMEOUT`, `BACKEND_HTTP_ERROR`, `BACKEND_UNAUTHORIZED`\n  - `DOCUMENTS_PATH_MISSING`, `INDEX_PARSE_ERROR`, `SECTION_NOT_FOUND`, `FILE_READ_ERROR`\n\n### Test Cases and Scenarios\n- Backend client tests:\n  - success, timeout, non-2xx, unauthorized token\n- Document tools tests:\n  - valid index load, filter behavior, missing section, malformed index, missing path\n- Domain tool integration smoke:\n  - one success call per module against known IDs\n  - forced failure verifies graceful envelope response\n- Acceptance checks:\n  - all Step 3 modules import cleanly\n  - every tool follows `{data, meta}` contract\n  - local + Docker behavior differs only by env values, not code path\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Phase 1 and Phase 2 are already complete and internal endpoints are available.\n- `DOCUMENTS_PATH` is mandatory (no auto-fallback path search).\n- Phase 4 subagents will consume tool `meta` to adjust confidence and user phrasing during degraded data states.\n"
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 291
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:09:07.651Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:09:07.651Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc5f-2041-74f0-93ef-99fc64440a80",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

`uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense && cat > agents/metrosense_agent/__init__.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom .agent import root_agent\\n\\n__all__ = [\\\"root_agent\\\"]\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/agent.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport os\\n\\nfrom google.adk.agents.llm_agent import Agent\\n\\nfrom .prompts import ROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION\\n\\nroot_agent = Agent(\\n    name=\\\"metrosense_agent\\\",\\n    model=os.getenv(\\\"AGENT_MODEL\\\", \\\"gemini-2.5-flash\\\"),\\n    description=\\\"MetroSense root agent package scaffold for domain tooling.\\\",\\n    instruction=ROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION,\\n    tools=[],\\n)\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/prompts.py <<'EOF'\\nROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION = (\\n    \\\"You are MetroSense. Route Bengaluru climate and infrastructure questions \\\"\\n    \\\"to appropriate sub-agents and use tool outputs with citations.\\\"\\n)\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/__init__.py <<'EOF'\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"Sub-agent package scaffold for Phase 4 implementation.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/chat_agent.py <<'EOF'\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"Chat agent implementation placeholder for Phase 4.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/flood_agent.py <<'EOF'\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"Flood vulnerability agent implementation placeholder for Phase 4.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/heat_agent.py <<'EOF'\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"Heat health agent implementation placeholder for Phase 4.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/infra_agent.py <<'EOF'\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"Infrastructure agent implementation placeholder for Phase 4.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/logistics_agent.py <<'EOF'\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"Logistics agent implementation placeholder for Phase 4.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_0KmIbm7rDIeGb9CAJSM5OsdP"
  }
}

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves.     "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

## Entry 328
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:11:43.280Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:11:43.280Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense && cat > agents/metrosense_agent/tools/types.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom typing import Any, Literal, TypedDict\\n\\nErrorCode = Literal[\\n    \\\"BACKEND_TIMEOUT\\\",\\n    \\\"BACKEND_HTTP_ERROR\\\",\\n    \\\"BACKEND_UNAUTHORIZED\\\",\\n    \\\"BACKEND_CONFIG_MISSING\\\",\\n    \\\"DOCUMENTS_PATH_MISSING\\\",\\n    \\\"INDEX_PARSE_ERROR\\\",\\n    \\\"SECTION_NOT_FOUND\\\",\\n    \\\"FILE_READ_ERROR\\\",\\n]\\n\\n\\nclass ToolMeta(TypedDict):\\n    ok: bool\\n    source: Literal[\\\"backend\\\", \\\"filesystem\\\"]\\n    error_code: ErrorCode | None\\n    error_detail: str | None\\n\\n\\nclass ToolResult(TypedDict):\\n    data: Any\\n    meta: ToolMeta\\n\\n\\ndef success_result(data: Any, source: Literal[\\\"backend\\\", \\\"filesystem\\\"]) -> ToolResult:\\n    return {\\n        \\\"data\\\": data,\\n        \\\"meta\\\": {\\n            \\\"ok\\\": True,\\n            \\\"source\\\": source,\\n            \\\"error_code\\\": None,\\n            \\\"error_detail\\\": None,\\n        },\\n    }\\n\\n\\ndef failure_result(\\n    source: Literal[\\\"backend\\\", \\\"filesystem\\\"],\\n    error_code: ErrorCode,\\n    error_detail: str,\\n    fallback_data: Any,\\n) -> ToolResult:\\n    return {\\n        \\\"data\\\": fallback_data,\\n        \\\"meta\\\": {\\n            \\\"ok\\\": False,\\n            \\\"source\\\": source,\\n            \\\"error_code\\\": error_code,\\n            \\\"error_detail\\\": error_detail,\\n        },\\n    }\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/tools/backend_client.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport os\\nfrom typing import Any\\n\\nimport httpx\\n\\nfrom .types import ToolResult, failure_result, success_result\\n\\n\\ndef _backend_base_url() -> str | None:\\n    base_url = os.getenv(\\\"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL\\\", \\\"\\\").strip()\\n    if not base_url:\\n        return None\\n    return base_url.rstrip(\\\"/\\\")\\n\\n\\ndef _internal_token() -> str | None:\\n    token = os.getenv(\\\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\\\", \\\"\\\").strip()\\n    if not token:\\n        return None\\n    return token\\n\\n\\nasync def _get(path: str, params: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> ToolResult:\\n    base_url = _backend_base_url()\\n    token = _internal_token()\\n    if not base_url or not token:\\n        return failure_result(\\n            source=\\\"backend\\\",\\n            error_code=\\\"BACKEND_CONFIG_MISSING\\\",\\n            error_detail=(\\n                \\\"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL and AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for backend tools\\\"\\n            ),\\n            fallback_data=[],\\n        )\\n\\n    headers = {\\\"X-Internal-Token\\\": token}\\n    url = f\\\"{base_url}{path}\\\"\\n\\n    try:\\n        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\\n            response = await client.get(url, params=params, headers=headers)\\n    except httpx.TimeoutException as exc:\\n        return failure_result(\\n            source=\\\"backend\\\",\\n            error_code=\\\"BACKEND_TIMEOUT\\\",\\n            error_detail=str(exc),\\n            fallback_data=[],\\n        )\\n    except httpx.HTTPError as exc:\\n        return failure_result(\\n            source=\\\"backend\\\",\\n            error_code=\\\"BACKEND_HTTP_ERROR\\\",\\n            error_detail=str(exc),\\n            fallback_data=[],\\n        )\\n\\n    if response.status_code == httpx.codes.UNAUTHORIZED:\\n        return failure_result(\\n            source=\\\"backend\\\",\\n            error_code=\\\"BACKEND_UNAUTHORIZED\\\",\\n            error_detail=response.text,\\n            fallback_data=[],\\n        )\\n\\n    if response.is_error:\\n        return failure_result(\\n            source=\\\"backend\\\",\\n            error_code=\\\"BACKEND_HTTP_ERROR\\\",\\n ToolResult\\n\\n\\nasync def get_traffic_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 5) -> ToolResult:\\n    return await _get(\\\"/internal/traffic/current\\\", {\\\"location_id\\\": location_id, \\\"limit\\\": limit})\\n\\n\\nasync def get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name: str, limit: int = 20) -> ToolResult:\\n    return await _get(\\\"/internal/traffic/corridor\\\", {\\\"corridor_name\\\": corridor_name, \\\"limit\\\": limit})\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/tools/__init__.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom .flood_tools import get_flood_incidents, get_lake_hydrology\\nfrom .heat_tools import get_aqi_current, get_aqi_historical, get_ward_profile\\nfrom .infra_tools import get_power_outage_events\\nfrom .logistics_tools import get_traffic_corridor, get_traffic_current\\nfrom .shared.document_tools import fetch_document_section, list_document_indexes\\nfrom .shared.location_tools import list_locations, resolve_location\\nfrom .shared.weather_tools import get_weather_current, get_weather_historical\\n\\n__all__ = [\\n    \\\"fetch_document_section\\\",\\n    \\\"get_aqi_current\\\",\\n    \\\"get_aqi_historical\\\",\\n    \\\"get_flood_incidents\\\",\\n    \\\"get_lake_hydrology\\\",\\n    \\\"get_power_outage_events\\\",\\n    \\\"get_traffic_corridor\\\",\\n    \\\"get_traffic_current\\\",\\n    \\\"get_weather_current\\\",\\n    \\\"get_weather_historical\\\",\\n    \\\"get_ward_profile\\\",\\n    \\\"list_document_indexes\\\",\\n    \\\"list_locations\\\",\\n    \\\"resolve_location\\\",\\n]\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/tools/shared/__init__.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom .document_tools import fetch_document_section, list_document_indexes\\nfrom .location_tools import list_locations, resolve_location\\nfrom .weather_tools import get_weather_current, get_weather_historical\\n\\n__all__ = [\\n    \\\"fetch_document_section\\\",\\n    \\\"get_weather_current\\\",\\n    \\\"get_weather_historical\\\",\\n    \\\"list_document_indexes\\\",\\n    \\\"list_locations\\\",\\n    \\\"resolve_location\\\",\\n]\\nEOF\"}",

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:12:02.621Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense && cat > agents/metrosense_agent/tests/__init__.py <<'EOF'\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"Tests for metrosense_agent package.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/tests/test_document_tools.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport os\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\nimport pytest\\n\\nfrom metrosense_agent.tools.shared.document_tools import fetch_document_section, list_document_indexes\\n\\n\\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\\nasync def test_list_document_indexes_missing_path(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:\\n    monkeypatch.delenv(\\\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\\\", raising=False)\\n    result = await list_document_indexes()\\n    assert result[\\\"meta\\\"][\\\"ok\\\"] is False\\n    assert result[\\\"meta\\\"][\\\"error_code\\\"] == \\\"DOCUMENTS_PATH_MISSING\\\"\\n\\n\\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\\nasync def test_list_document_indexes_filters(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:\\n    docs_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / \\\"server\\\" / \\\"Documents_Metrosense\\\"\\n    monkeypatch.setenv(\\\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\\\", str(docs_path))\\n\\n    result = await list_document_indexes(document_type=\\\"drainage_plan\\\", ward_name=\\\"Bellandur\\\")\\n    assert result[\\\"meta\\\"][\\\"ok\\\"] is True\\n    assert isinstance(result[\\\"data\\\"], list)\\n    assert len(result[\\\"data\\\"]) >= 1\\n\\n\\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\\nasync def test_fetch_document_section_success(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:\\n    docs_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / \\\"server\\\" / \\\"Documents_Metrosense\\\"\\n    monkeypatch.setenv(\\\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\\\", str(docs_path))\\n\\n    result = await fetch_document_section(\\\"drainage_plan_bbmp_2019\\\", \\\"s2\\\")\\n    assert result[\\\"meta\\\"][\\\"ok\\\"] is True\\n    assert result[\\\"data\\\"][\\\"document_id\\\"] == \\\"drainage_plan_bbmp_2019\\\"\\n    assert \\\"content\\\" in result[\\\"data\\\"]\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/tests/test_backend_client.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport pytest\\n\\nfrom metrosense_agent.tools.backend_client import _get\\n\\n\\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\\nasync def test_backend_config_missing(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:\\n    monkeypatch.delenv(\\\"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL\\\", raising=False)\\n    monkeypatch.delenv(\\\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\\\", raising=False)\\n\\n    result = await _get(\\\"/internal/weather/current\\\", {\\\"location_id\\\": \\\"loc_001\\\"})\\n    assert result[\\\"meta\\\"][\\\"ok\\\"] is False\\n    assert result[\\\"meta\\\"][\\\"error_code\\\"] == \\\"BACKEND_CONFIG_MISSING\\\"\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > agents/metrosense_agent/tests/test_domain_tools.py <<'EOF'\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport pytest\\n\\nfrom metrosense_agent.tools.flood_tools import get_flood_incidents\\nfrom metrosense_agent.tools.heat_tools import get_aqi_current\\nfrom metrosense_agent.tools.infra_tools import get_power_outage_events\\nfrom metrosense_agent.tools.logistics_tools import get_traffic_current\\nfrom metrosense_agent.tools.shared.location_tools import resolve_location\\nfrom metrosense_agent.tools.shared.weather_tools import get_weather_current\\n\\n\\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\\n@pytest.mark.parametrize(\\n    \\\"tool_call\\\",\\n    [\\n        lambda: get_weather_current(\\\"loc_001\\\"),\\n        lambda: resolve_location(\\\"Bellandur\\\"),\\n        lambda: get_flood_incidents(\\\"loc_001\\\"),\\n        lambda: get_aqi_current(\\\"loc_001\\\"),\\n        lambda: get_power_outage_events(\\\"loc_001\\\"),\\n        lambda: get_traffic_current(\\\"loc_001\\\"),\\n    ],\\n)\\nasync def test_domain_tools_return_contract(\\n    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,\\n    tool_call,\\n) -> None:\\n    monkeypatch.delenv(\\\"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL\\\", raising=False)\\n    monkeypatch.delenv(\\\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\\\", raising=False)\\n\\n    result = await tool_call()\\n    assert \\\"data\\\" in result\\n    assert \\\"meta\\\" in result\\n    assert result[\\\"meta\\\"][\\\"ok\\\"] is False\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_dslJQ1F3JscWXWOZoijwQmh3"
  }
}

**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:14:01.294Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_UKWgUt3WZEoI9bP10F5CuTyy",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 10d697\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1918\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport httpx\n\nfrom .types import ToolResult, failure_result, success_result\n\n\ndef _backend_base_url() -> str | None:\n    base_url = os.getenv(\"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL\", \"\").strip()\n    if not base_url:\n        return None\n    return base_url.rstrip(\"/\")\n\n\ndef _internal_token() -> str | None:\n    token = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\").strip()\n    if not token:\n        return None\n    return token\n\n\nasync def _get(path: str, params: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> ToolResult:\n    base_url = _backend_base_url()\n    token = _internal_token()\n    if not base_url or not token:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"backend\",\n            error_code=\"BACKEND_CONFIG_MISSING\",\n            error_detail=(\n                \"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL and AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for backend tools\"\n            ),\n            fallback_data=[],\n        )\n\n    headers = {\"X-Internal-Token\": token}\n    url = f\"{base_url}{path}\"\n\n    try:\n        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n            response = await client.get(url, params=params, headers=headers)\n    except httpx.TimeoutException as exc:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"backend\",\n            error_code=\"BACKEND_TIMEOUT\",\n            error_detail=str(exc),\n            fallback_data=[],\n        )\n    except httpx.HTTPError as exc:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"backend\",\n            error_code=\"BACKEND_HTTP_ERROR\",\n            error_detail=str(exc),\n            fallback_data=[],\n        )\n\n    if response.status_code == httpx.codes.UNAUTHORIZED:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"backend\",\n            error_code=\"BACKEND_UNAUTHORIZED\",\n            error_detail=response.text,\n            fallback_data=[],\n        )\n\n    if response.is_error:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"backend\",\n            error_code=\"BACKEND_HTTP_ERROR\",\n            error_detail=f\"{response.status_code}: {response.text}\",\n            fallback_data=[],\n        )\n\n    try:\n        payload = response.json()\n    except ValueError as exc:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"backend\",\n            error_code=\"BACKEND_HTTP_ERROR\",\n            error_detail=f\"Invalid JSON response: {exc}\",\n            fallback_data=[],\n        )\n\n    return success_result(payload, source=\"backend\")\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport os\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom ..types import ToolResult, failure_result, success_result\n\n\ndef _documents_path() -> Path | None:\n    raw_path = os.getenv(\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\", \"\").strip()\n    if not raw_path:\n        return None\n    return Path(raw_path)\n\n\ndef _load_indexes(base_path: Path) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], ToolResult | None]:\n    index_files = sorted(base_path.glob(\"*_index.json\"))\n    indexes: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n\n    for path in index_files:\n        try:\n            indexes.append(json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")))\n        except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:\n            return [], failure_result(\n                source=\"filesystem\",\n                error_code=\"INDEX_PARSE_ERROR\",\n                error_detail=f\"Failed to parse index '{path.name}': {exc}\",\n                fallback_data=[],\n            )\n\n    return indexes, None\n\n\ndef _contains_thresholds(index_doc: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:\n    toc = index_doc.get(\"table_of_contents\", [])\n    if not isinstance(toc, list):\n        return False\n    return any(bool(section.get(\"contains_thresholds\")) for section in toc if isinstance(section, dict))\n\n\nasync def list_document_indexes(\n    document_type: str | None = None,\n    ward_name: str | None = None,\n    contains_thresholds: bool | None = None,\n) -> ToolResult:\n    base_path = _documents_path()\n    if base_path is None or not base_path.exists() or not base_path.is_dir():\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"filesystem\",\n            error_code=\"DOCUMENTS_PATH_MISSING\",\n            error_detail=\"DOCUMENTS_PATH is missing or does not point to a readable directory\",\n            fallback_data=[],\n        )\n\n    indexes, error = _load_indexes(base_path)\n    if error is not None:\n        return error\n\n    filtered: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    ward_lookup = (ward_name or \"\").strip().lower()\n    requested_type = (document_type or \"\").strip().lower()\n\n    for idx in indexes:\n        if requested_type and str(idx.get(\"document_type\", \"\")).lower() != requested_type:\n            continue\n\n        if ward_lookup:\n            wards = idx.get(\"wards_covered\", [])\n            if not isinstance(wards, list):\n                continue\n            if ward_lookup not in {str(ward).strip().lower() for ward in wards}:\n                continue\n\n        if contains_thresholds is not None and _contains_thresholds(idx) != contains_thresholds:\n            continue\n\n        filtered.append(idx)\n\n    return success_result(filtered, source=\"filesystem\")\n\n\nasync def fetch_document_section(document_id: str, section_id: str) -> ToolResult:\n    base_path = _documents_path()\n    if base_path is None or not base_path.exists() or not base_path.is_dir():\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"filesystem\",\n            error_code=\"DOCUMENTS_PATH_MISSING\",\n            error_detail=\"DOCUMENTS_PATH is missing or does not point to a readable directory\",\n            fallback_data=None,\n        )\n\n    indexes, error = _load_indexes(base_path)\n    if error is not None:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"filesystem\",\n            error_code=\"INDEX_PARSE_ERROR\",\n            error_detail=error[\"meta\"][\"error_detail\"] or \"Index parse failed\",\n            fallback_data=None,\n        )\n\n    document: dict[str, Any] | None = None\n    section: dict[str, Any] | None = None\n\n    for idx in indexes:\n        if str(idx.get(\"document_id\")) != document_id:\n            continue\n\n        toc = idx.get(\"table_of_contents\", [])\n        if not isinstance(toc, list):\n            continue\n\n        for item in toc:\n            if isinstance(item, dict) and str(item.get(\"section_id\")) == section_id:\n                document = idx\n                section = item\n                break\n        if section is not None:\n            break\n\n    if document is None or section is None:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"filesystem\",\n            error_code=\"SECTION_NOT_FOUND\",\n            error_detail=f\"Section '{section_id}' not found in document '{document_id}'\",\n            fallback_data=None,\n        )\n\n    text_file = section.get(\"text_file\")\n    if not text_file:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"filesystem\",\n            error_code=\"FILE_READ_ERROR\",\n            error_detail=f\"Section '{section_id}' in '{document_id}' has no text_file reference\",\n            fallback_data=None,\n        )\n\n    section_path = base_path / str(text_file)\n\n    try:\n        content = section_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except OSError as exc:\n        return failure_result(\n            source=\"filesystem\",\n            error_code=\"FILE_READ_ERROR\",\n            error_detail=f\"Failed to read section file '{section_path.name}': {exc}\",\n            fallback_data=None,\n        )\n\n    payload = {\n        \"document_id\": document_id,\n        \"section_id\": section_id,\n        \"title\": section.get(\"title\"),\n        \"summary\": section.get(\"summary\"),\n        \"page_start\": section.get(\"page_start\"),\n        \"page_end\": section.get(\"page_end\"),\n        \"text_file\": str(text_file),\n        \"content\": content,\n    }\n    return success_result(payload, source=\"filesystem\")\n"
  }
}

## Entry 409
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:16:35.171Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:16:35.171Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Implemented Phase 3 tooling and file structure under `agents/metrosense_agent`.\n\n### What I added\n- New package scaffold:\n  - [metrosense_agent/__init__.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/__init__.py)\n  - [metrosense_agent/agent.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/agent.py)\n  - [metrosense_agent/prompts.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/prompts.py)\n  - `subagents/*` placeholder files for Phase 4.\n\n- Full tools layer with shared contract:\n  - [tools/types.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/types.py)  \n    `ToolResult = {data, meta}` with error codes.\n  - [tools/backend_client.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/backend_client.py)  \n    token-authenticated async HTTP client for `/internal/*`.\n  - [tools/shared/document_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/shared/document_tools.py)  \n    strict `DOCUMENTS_PATH` filesystem access, filters, section fetch.\n  - [tools/shared/weather_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/shared/weather_tools.py)\n  - [tools/shared/location_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/shared/location_tools.py)\n  - [tools/flood_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/flood_tools.py)\n  - [tools/heat_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/heat_tools.py)\n  - [tools/infra_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/infra_tools.py)\n  - [tools/logistics_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/logistics_tools.py)\n  - export surfaces in [tools/__init__.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/__init__.py) and `tools/shared/__init__.py`.\n\n- Tests added:\n  - [tests/test_backend_client.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tests/test_backend_client.py)\n  - [tests/test_document_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tests/test_document_tools.py)\n  - [tests/test_domain_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tests/test_domain_tools.py)\n\n### Config/dependency updates\n- Added required env vars in [agents/.env.example](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/.env.example):\n  - `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL`\n  - `DOCUMENTS_PATH`\n- Added dependencies in [agents/pyproject.toml](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/pyproject.toml):\n  - `python-dotenv>=1.0`\n  - `pydantic>=2.7`\n- `agents/uv.lock` updated accordingly.\n\n### Validation run\n- `python3 -m compileall metrosense_agent` passed.\n- `uv run` sanity check passed with:\n  - backend config-missing path returns graceful `meta.error_code=BACKEND_CONFIG_MISSING`\n  - document index listing works with `DOCUMENTS_PATH=../server/Documents_Metrosense`\n  - section fetch works (`drainage_plan_bbmp_2019`, `s2`).\n\nNotes:\n- I did not modify unrelated existing dirty/untracked files outside this scope.\n- Phase 4 can now consume these tools directly with the `{data, meta}` contract.\n\nNext natural step:\n1. Wire these tools into real subagent implementations (`chat/flood/heat/infra/logistics`) and replace placeholder files.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```
