ADK Runtime and Agent Hierarchy\n\n### How ADK Runs the System\n\nThe ADK Runner is not a designed component — it is ADK infrastructure. It executes the Root Agent, manages the tool call event loop, and returns the final response. Configuration is a single line:\n\n```python\nrunner = Runner(agent=root_agent, session_service=session_service)\nrunner.run(user_message)\n```\n\n### Agent Hierarchy\n\n```\nADK Runner\n    │\n    ▼\nRoot Agent (Orchestrator)\n    │   — Intent classification\n    │   — Parallel dispatch via asyncio.gather()\n    │   — Level-1 → Level-2 scorecard merge\n    │   — Global guardrails\n    │\n    ▼\nChat Agent  ← only agent the user ever talks to\n    │   — Input guardrails\n    │   — Session memory read/write\n    │   — Fan-out to domain sub-agents\n    │   — NL synthesis with citations\n    │   — Chart follow-up trigger\n    │   — Scorecard mode trigger\n    │\n    ├── Flood Vulnerability Agent     (sub-agent)\n    ├── Urban Heat & Health Agent     (sub-agent)\n    ├── Infrastructure Stress Agent   (sub-agent)\n    └── Logistics Disruption Agent    (sub-agent)\n```\n\n### Key Design Principles\n\n**Runner is infrastructure, not an agent.** Do not design a runner agent — it is a one-line ADK configuration.\n\n**Root Agent never talks to the user.** It routes, dispatches, and merges. The Chat Agent owns all user-facing communication.\n\n**Chat Agent calls domain agents directly.** No round-trip back through Root Agent for domain queries. Chat Agent fans out directly, collects Level-1 payloads, and synthesises the NL response.\n\n**Guardrails live at the Chat Agent.** Because Chat Agent is the actual entry point for user input, input filtering and injection detection live here — not assumed to be handled upstream.\n\n**Sub-agents are registered under Chat Agent** and are pure domain reasoning units. They never receive user input directly.\n\n### Sub-Agent Future-Proofing\n\nSub-agents are not used in v1 for simplicity. The signals to introduce them later:\n- Any domain agent tool count exceeds 10-12 and LLM tool selection degrades\n- Any agent file exceeds ~300-400 lines\n- Tool logic is being copy-pasted across two domain agents\n\nTool files are pre-partitioned by concern inside each agent folder so splitting is a mechanical refactor when the time comes.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Agent Responsibilities\n\n### Root Agent (Orchestrator)\n\n**Owns:**\n- Intent classification — maps NL query to one or more domain intents\n- Output mode determination — text / scorecard / chart follow-up\n- Parallel dispatch — `asyncio.gather()` for multi-domain queries\n- Level-1 payload collection and pure Python merge → Level-2 Unified Scorecard\n- Global guardrails before final response\n\n**State Bag:**\n```python\n{\n  \"query_id\": \"uuid\",\n  \"intent\": [\"flood_vulnerability\", \"logistics_disruption\"],\n  \"active_agents\": [\"flood\", \"logistics\"],\n  \"partial_results\": {},\n  \"session_history\": [],\n  \"output_mode\": \"text | scorecard | chart\",\n  \"data_freshness\": {}\n}\n```\n\n**Does not own:**\n- Conversation with user\n- Session memory\n- NL synthesis\n\n---\n\n### Chat Agent\n\n**Owns:**\n- The only conversational interface — user input comes in here, NL response goes out here\n- Input guardrails — domain relevance, injection detection, query length limits\n- Session memory read and write\n- Location resolution — maps \"Bellandur\" or \"HSR Layout Sector 2\" to `location_id` via `location_master`\n- Fan-out to domain sub-agents directly\n- NL response synthesis with citations\n- Chart follow-up evaluation and prompt injection\n- Scorecard mode trigger on NL keywords\n\n**Routing examples:**\n```\n\"If 60mm tonight, which underpasses?\"         → Flood + Logistics (parallel)\n\"Compare AQI vs decade average this month\"    → Heat Agent (historical mode)\n\"Tree-fall risk near MG Road?\"                → Infra Agent (zone filter)\n\"Risk scorecard for Bellandur\"                → All agents → Unified Scorecard\n\"Will ORR delay cross 1.5x tonight?\"          → Logistics Agent only\n```\n\n**Chart follow-up behavior:**\nAfter any trend or comparative answer, Chat Agent evaluates whether a chart would genuinely improve understanding. If yes it appends: *\"Would you like to see this as a chart?\"* Charts are never auto-generated — explicit user confirmation required.\n\n**Scorecard trigger keywords:**\n`risk`, `scorecard`, `vulnerability`, `assessment`, `how vulnerable is [place]`\nNote: `summary` is explicitly excluded — too generic, used in non-scorecard contexts.\n\n---\n\n### Flood Vulnerability Agent\n\n**Goal:** Identify flood black spots, predict inundation risk, recommend barricade locations.\n\n**Postgres Tools:**\n```\nget_rainfall_current(location_id)\nget_rainfall_historical(location_id, window_hours)\nget_lake_hydrology(lake_id)\nget_flood_incidents_historical(location_id)\n```\n\n**Document Tools:**\n```\nlist_document_indexes(document_type=\"drainage_plan\", ward_name)\nlist_document_indexes(document_type=\"flood_postmortem\", ward_name)\nlist_document_indexes(document_type=\"imd_bulletin\", ward_name)\nfetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)\n```\n\n**Key reasoning logic:**\n- `fill_percentage` + `overflow_status` + `surplus_flow_cusecs` from `lake_hydrology` drives backflow risk\n- `rainfall_intensity_mm_hr` vs drainage capacity extracted from document sections is the flash flood trigger\n- Barricade threshold values (mm/hr) come from drainage plan document sections, not hardcoded\n- 2022 flood post-mortem sections provide calibration priors for black spot scoring\n- Pre-seeded monitored zones: Sarjapur, Bellandur, Manyata Tech Park, Varthur, Hebbal, KR Puram, Koramangala, Silk Board\n\n**Level-1 Output:**\n```json\n{\n  \"agent\": \"flood_vulnerability\",\n  \"confidence\": 0.87,\n  \"status\": \"ok\",\n  \"data\": {\n    \"zone_scores\": [],\n    \"blackspots\": [],\n    \"barricade_recommendations\": [],\n    \"lake_status\": {}\n  },\n  \"citations\": [],\n  \"data_freshness\": {}\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Urban Heat & Health Agent\n\n**Goal:** Generate neighbourhood-specific health advisories from AQI data and climate research documents.\n\n**Postgres Tools:**\n```\nget_aqi_current(location_id)\nget_aqi_historical(location_id, days)\nget_weather_heat_metrics(location_id)\nget_population_vulnerability(ward_id)     -- queries ward_profile table\n```\n\n**Document Tools:**\n```\nlist_document_indexes(document_type=\"resilience_paper\", ward_name)\nlist_document_indexes(document_type=\"imd_bulletin\", ward_name)\nfetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)\n```\n\n**Key reasoning logic:**\n- `aqi_category` + `dominant_pollutant` generates specific advisory — not generic messaging\n- `elderly_population_pct` + `children_population_pct` from `ward_profile` flags sensitive population zones\n- `green_cover_pct` from `ward_profile` is the UHI intensity multiplier\n- WHO AQI thresholds applied per pollutant type\n- Temperature + humidity drives heat stress tier reasoning\n\n**Level-1 Output:**\n```json\n{\n  \"agent\": \"heat_health\",\n  \"confidence\": 0.82,\n  \"status\": \"ok\",\n  \"data\": {\n    \"zone_aqi\": [],\n    \"heat_stress_tier\": \"HIGH | MODERATE | LOW\",\n    \"advisories\": [],\n    \"population_risk_zones\": []\n  },\n  \"citations\": [],\n  \"data_freshness\": {}\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Infrastructure Stress Agent\n\n**Goal:** Predict tree-fall likelihood and power grid failure probability from wind telemetry and document-extracted tree density and outage history.\n\n**Postgres Tools:**\n```\nget_wind_current(location_id)\nget_wind_historical(location_id, window_hours)\nget_power_outage_events(location_id, window_days)\n```\n\n**Document Tools:**\n```\nlist_document_indexes(document_type=\"tree_density_map\", ward_name)\nlist_document_indexes(document_type=\"bescom_report\", ward_name)\nfetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)\n```\n\n**Key reasoning logic:**\n- `wind_gust_kmh`, not average wind speed, is the primary tree-fall predictor\n- Tree density and fall-prone species data comes from BBMP tree density map document sections\n- `fault_type = treefall` in `power_outage_event` is the direct calibration signal for the scoring model\n- `outage_type = planned` is excluded from risk scores — only unplanned outages are relevant\n- Powerline proximity data extracted from BESCOM document sections\n\n**Level-1 Output:**\n```json\n{\n  \"agent\": \"infrastructure\",\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 439
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:20:08.781Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:20:08.781Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_OVzdMDfliDxMQ7oVinbKTDH4",
    "output": "Chunk ID: f804c8\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 4074\nOutput:\n```\n\nAfter extracting `message_text` from ADK events:\n1. Call `conversation_service.upsert_session(db_session, session_id)`\n2. Call `conversation_service.append_turn(db_session, session_id, message, message_text, agents_invoked=[], latency_ms=<elapsed>)`\n3. `await db_session.commit()`\n\nThe chat route in `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` already gets `db_session` injected via `Depends(db_session)` — pass it through to `get_chat_response`.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Agent Architecture\n\n### 5.1 Agent hierarchy (exact ADK structure)\n\n```\nroot_agent  (LlmAgent, name=\"root_agent\")\n    sub_agents:\n        └── chat_agent  (LlmAgent, name=\"chat_agent\")\n                sub_agents:\n                    ├── flood_vulnerability_agent\n                    ├── heat_health_agent\n                    ├── infrastructure_agent\n                    └── logistics_agent\n```\n\nADK `sub_agents` are registered as a list on the parent agent. The parent can transfer control to them via tool call or explicit hand-off. The root_agent routes to chat_agent for all domain traffic.\n\n### 5.2 `metrosense_agent/agent.py` — root_agent\n\n```python\nfrom google.adk.agents import Agent\nfrom metrosense_agent.subagents.chat_agent import chat_agent\nfrom metrosense_agent.prompts import ROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION\n\nroot_agent = Agent(\n    name=\"root_agent\",\n    model=os.getenv(\"AGENT_MODEL\", \"gemini-2.5-flash\"),\n    description=\"MetroSense root orchestrator — routes all queries to the chat agent.\",\n    instruction=ROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION,\n    sub_agents=[chat_agent],\n)\n```\n\nroot_agent instruction:\n- Never answer directly. Always delegate to chat_agent.\n- Domain: Bengaluru climate, flood, AQI, power, traffic only.\n- Reject non-Bengaluru geography queries before delegation.\n- Reject harmful / non-domain queries with a fixed refusal string.\n\n### 5.3 `subagents/chat_agent.py` — chat_agent\n\nThe only agent that synthesises a natural language response to the user.\n\nTools on chat_agent: `resolve_location`, `list_document_indexes`\n\nSub-agents: `flood_vulnerability_agent`, `heat_health_agent`, `infrastructure_agent`, `logistics_agent`\n\nInstruction responsibilities:\n1. **Input guardrail** — if the query is not about Bengaluru climate/infrastructure, respond with: `\"I can only answer questions about Bengaluru's climate, flood risk, air quality, power grid, and traffic. Please rephrase.\"`\n2. **Location resolution** — always call `resolve_location` before forwarding to domain agents. Canonical `location_id` must be established.\n3. **Routing** — map intents to agents:\n   - Flood / lake / inundation / drainage → `flood_vulnerability_agent`\n   - AQI / air quality / health / heat / UHI → `heat_health_agent`\n   - Power / outage / BESCOM / tree fall / wind → `infrastructure_agent`\n   - Traffic / corridor / ORR / delay / logistics → `logistics_agent`\n   - Multi-domain → delegate to multiple agents in sequence, then synthesise\n4. **Scorecard trigger** — if query contains `risk`, `scorecard`, `vulnerability`, `assessment`, `how vulnerable is` → call ALL four domain agents and synthesise the Unified Risk Scorecard format.\n5. **Citation rule** — every factual claim in the response must reference either a DB record timestamp or a document section ID. No unsourced numerical claims.\n6. **Data freshness** — include the observed_at timestamp of the most recent data point used in the response.\n\n### 5.4 `subagents/flood_agent.py` — flood_vulnerability_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — from `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_historical(location_id, hours)` — from `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n- `get_lake_hydrology(lake_id, limit)` — from `tools/flood_tools.py`\n- `get_flood_incidents(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/flood_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — from `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — from `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n\nInstruction:\n- Primary signal: `rainfall_intensity_mm_hr` from weather + `fill_percentage` + `overflow_status` from lake hydrology\n- Secondary signal: `flood_incident` history for the location (frequency, severity, water_depth_cm)\n- Document grounding: read flood study index → identify relevant sections (ones with `contains_thresholds: true` for the ward) → fetch section → extract threshold values → compare against live data\n- Barricade recommendation: only if `rainfall_intensity_mm_hr > threshold_from_document` AND (`fill_percentage > 85` OR `overflow_status = true`)\n- Output in structured format (see §7)\n- Known monitored lake IDs (from golden data): Bellandur, Varthur, KR Puram, Hebbal — map these from location resolution\n\n### 5.5 `subagents/heat_agent.py` — heat_health_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_aqi_current(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `get_aqi_historical(location_id, days)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared weather tool (temperature, humidity)\n- `get_ward_profile(ward_id)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- `aqi_value` + `aqi_category` + `dominant_pollutant` drive advisory specificity\n- `elderly_population_pct` + `children_population_pct` from ward_profile → sensitive population multiplier\n- `green_cover_pct` → UHI intensity factor\n- WHO thresholds applied per pollutant type (these are embedded in instruction, not from DB)\n- Requires specific advisory text per pollutant: PM2.5 → \"avoid outdoor exercise\", NO2 → \"keep windows closed\", etc.\n\n### 5.6 `subagents/infra_agent.py` — infrastructure_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared\n- `get_weather_historical(location_id, hours)` — shared (wind gust trend)\n- `get_power_outage_events(location_id, window_days)` — from `tools/infra_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- Primary signal: `wind_gust_kmh` (NOT `wind_speed_kmh`) from most recent weather observation\n- Only `outage_type != 'planned'` events are used for historical calibration\n- `fault_type = 'treefall'` events are the direct tree-fall risk signal\n- Document: BBMP tree canopy reference → tree density for the ward\n- Document: BESCOM infra reference → powerline proximity data for the ward\n- Risk tiers: `wind_gust_kmh < 40` = LOW, `40–60` = MODERATE, `60–80` = HIGH, `> 80` = CRITICAL (instruction-embedded, then calibrated by historical outage frequency)\n\n### 5.7 `subagents/logistics_agent.py` — logistics_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_traffic_current(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n- `get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name, limit)` — from `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared\n- `get_flood_incidents(location_id, limit)` — flood tools (waterlogging signals)\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- `congestion_index` + `delay_minutes` + `waterlogging_flag` are the primary traffic signals\n- `rainfall_mm_1h_at_time` on traffic observations provides the rain-at-observation context\n- `heavy_vehicle_share` matters for ORR and Hosur Road degradation rate\n- Document: flood study s4 (delay multipliers by corridor and rainfall band) → use for calibration\n- Key corridors: ORR, Sarjapur Road, Hosur Road, Bellary Road, Mysore Road, KR Puram\n\n---\n\n## 6. Tool Implementations\n\n### 6.1 `tools/backend_client.py`\n\nSingle `httpx.AsyncClient`-based module. Reads `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL` and `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` from environment.\n\n```python\nBACKEND_URL = os.getenv(\"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL\", \"http://localhost:8010\")\nINTERNAL_TOKEN = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\")\n\nasync def _get(path: str, params: dict) -> list[dict] | dict | None:\n    \"\"\"Single internal HTTP GET with token auth. Raises on non-2xx.\"\"\"\n```\n\nAll tool functions call `_get()`. On `httpx.TimeoutException` or HTTP error → return empty list `[]` (tools must be fault-tolerant — a data source being unavailable should not crash the agent, just reduce answer quality).\n\nTimeout: `10.0` seconds per tool call.\n\n### 6.2 `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_weather_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 1) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get most recent weather observations for a location.\"\"\"\n\nasync def get_weather_historical(location_id: str, hours: int = 24) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get weather observations for the past N hours for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.3 `tools/shared/location_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def resolve_location(name: str) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Resolve a free-text location name (e.g. 'Bellandur', 'HSR Layout Sector 2')\n    to canonical location_master entries.\n    Returns list of matches with location_id, canonical_name, ward_id, ward_name.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def list_locations() -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"List all known Bangalore locations in the system.\"\"\"\n```\n\n`resolve_location` hits `GET /internal/locations/resolve?name=<name>`.\n\n### 6.4 `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n\nDocument tools work **entirely from the filesystem** — no backend HTTP call. They read the mounted volume at `DOCUMENTS_PATH`.\n\n```python\n```python\nDOCUMENTS_PATH = Path(os.getenv(\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\", \"/documents\"))\n\nasync def list_document_indexes(\n    document_type: str | None = None,\n    ward_name: str | None = None,\n) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    List available document indexes.\n    If document_type is given, filter by index['document_type'].\n    If ward_name is given, filter to indexes whose wards_covered contains ward_name.\n    Returns list of index metadata (without full table_of_contents content — just section summaries).\n    Agents use this to decide WHICH document and section to fetch before calling fetch_document_section.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def fetch_document_section(\n    document_id: str,\n    section_id: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Read and return the text of one section from a document.\n    document_id maps to the *_index.json file (e.g. 'bangalore_flood_study_2022').\n    section_id is the section_id field in the index's table_of_contents (e.g. 's2').\n    Returns the full markdown text of that section.\n    \"\"\"\n```\n\n`list_document_indexes` reads all `*_index.json` files from `DOCUMENTS_PATH`.\n`fetch_document_section` reads the `text_file` path from the matching index entry.\n\nThese functions are `async def` but use `asyncio.to_thread` for the filesystem reads (non-blocking).\n\n### 6.5 `tools/flood_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_lake_hydrology(lake_id: str, limit: int = 5) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get most recent lake hydrology observations for a lake.\"\"\"\n\nasync def get_flood_incidents(location_id: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get recent flood incidents for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.6 `tools/heat_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_aqi_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 1) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_aqi_historical(location_id: str, days: int = 7) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_ward_profile(ward_id: str) -> dict | None:\n```\n\n### 6.7 `tools/infra_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_power_outage_events(location_id: str, window_days: int = 30) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get unplanned power outage events within the past N days for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.8 `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_traffic_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 5) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict]:\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Prompts (`prompts.py`)\n\nAll instruction strings live here. Never inline strings in agent files. Structure:\n\n```python\nROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nCHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nFLOOD_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nHEAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nINFRA_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nLOGISTICS_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\n```\n\nEach instruction includes:\n- Role and domain scope\n- Explicit list of tools available (agents reason better when told what they can call)\n- Output format for Level-1 domain payload (strict typed JSON envelope)\n- Citation requirements\n- Data freshness: include `observed_at` of the most recent record used\n- Context requirements: include resolved location, time window, and key assumptions in `context_used`\n\n### Guardrail strings embedded in CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION\n\n```\nOFF_DOMAIN_REFUSAL = \"I can only answer questions about Bengaluru's climate, flood risk, \nair quality, power grid, and traffic. Please rephrase your question.\"\n\nLOCATION_NOT_FOUND = \"I couldn't find '{name}' in the Bengaluru location registry. \nPlease use a ward name or neighbourhood (e.g. 'Bellandur', 'Koramangala', 'Whitefield').\"\n\nNO_DATA_AVAILABLE = \"No recent data is available for {location} on {domain}. \nThe dataset covers historical records — please check the data freshness.\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Session Persistence Flow\n\n```\nUser → POST /api/chat\n    │\n    ▼\nchat route (FastAPI)\n    │ ─── calls ──→ agent_proxy.get_chat_response(settings, db_session, session_id, message)\n    │                   │\n    │                   ├─ 1. _ensure_session (ADK InMemory)\n    │                   ├─ 2. POST /run → ADK → root_agent → chat_agent → domain agents\n    │                   ├─ 3. map ADK output to canonical Level-2 response contract\n    │                   ├─ 4. conversation_service.upsert_session(db_session, session_id)\n    │                   ├─ 5. conversation_service.append_turn(db_session, session_id, ...)\n    │                   └─ 6. await db_session.commit()\n    │\n    ▼\nLevel-2 response returned to user\n```\n\nThe DB write happens **after** the ADK call succeeds. If the DB write fails, the response is still returned to the user (log the error, don't fail the request). This is a best-effort persistence approach — data availability > perfect persistence.\n\n### 8.1 Canonical response contracts (Level-1 vs Level-2)\n\nLevel-1 is internal and mandatory for every domain agent call. Level-2 is the only backend response shape exposed to frontend clients.\n\n**Level-1 domain payload (internal)**\n- Strict typed schema with required fields: `agent`, `query_id`, `timestamp`, `status`, `confidence`, `data`, `citations`, `data_freshness`, `context_used`, `errors`\n- `status` enum is `ok | partial | error`\n- `context_used` is required to capture resolved location, intent, and operational assumptions\n- L1 payloads are not rendered directly in UI\n\n**Level-2 user delivery contract (backend -> frontend)**\n- Single stable shape for all chat responses:\n  - `session_id`\n  - `response_mode` (`text | scorecard | artifact`)\n  - `response_text` (always present)\n  - `citations_summary` (always present, may be empty)\n  - `data_freshness_summary` (always present)\n  - `risk_card` (nullable)\n  - `artifact` (nullable)\n  - `follow_up_prompt` (nullable)\n- `risk_card` and `artifact` are populated only when the user explicitly asks and the capability is implemented; otherwise they remain `null`\n- Mapping from Level-1 -> Level-2 is deterministic (no separate formatting agent)\n\nExample Level-2 response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n  \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n  \"response_text\": \"Bellandur flood risk is elevated tonight due to rainfall and lake fill conditions.\",\n  \"citations_summary\": [],\n  \"data_freshness_summary\": {\n    \"telemetry_lag_seconds\": 42,\n    \"stale_sources\": []\n  },\n  \"risk_card\": null,\n  \"artifact\": null,\n  \"follow_up_prompt\": null\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Environment Variables\n\n### Backend additions (`server/.env`)\n\nNo new variables needed — the backend already has `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN`. The internal routes use it for verification.\n\n### Agents additions (`agents/.env`)\n\n```\nBACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://localhost:8010    # or http://server:8010 in Docker\nDOCUMENTS_PATH=/documents                     # mounted volume path\nAGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token       # same as backend\nAGENT_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash\nGOOGLE_API_KEY=<your_key>\n```\n\n### Docker Compose changes (agents service)\n\n```yaml\nagents:\n  ...\n  environment:\n    - BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://server:8010\n    - DOCUMENTS_PATH=/documents\n"
  }
}
```

{
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_OVzdMDfliDxMQ7oVinbKTDH4",
    "output": "Chunk ID: f804c8\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 4074\nOutput:\n```\n\nAfter extracting `message_text` from ADK events:\n1. Call `conversation_service.upsert_session(db_session, session_id)`\n2. Call `conversation_service.append_turn(db_session, session_id, message, message_text, agents_invoked=[], latency_ms=<elapsed>)`\n3. `await db_session.commit()`\n\nThe chat route in `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` already gets `db_session` injected via `Depends(db_session)` — pass it through to `get_chat_response`.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Agent Architecture\n\n### 5.1 Agent hierarchy (exact ADK structure)\n\n```\nroot_agent  (LlmAgent, name=\"root_agent\")\n    sub_agents:\n        └── chat_agent  (LlmAgent, name=\"chat_agent\")\n                sub_agents:\n                    ├── flood_vulnerability_agent\n                    ├── heat_health_agent\n                    ├── infrastructure_agent\n                    └── logistics_agent\n```\n\nADK `sub_agents` are registered as a list on the parent agent. The parent can transfer control to them via tool call or explicit hand-off. The root_agent routes to chat_agent for all domain traffic.\n\n### 5.2 `metrosense_agent/agent.py` — root_agent\n\n```python\nfrom google.adk.agents import Agent\nfrom metrosense_agent.subagents.chat_agent import chat_agent\nfrom metrosense_agent.prompts import ROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION\n\nroot_agent = Agent(\n    name=\"root_agent\",\n    model=os.getenv(\"AGENT_MODEL\", \"gemini-2.5-flash\"),\n    description=\"MetroSense root orchestrator — routes all queries to the chat agent.\",\n    instruction=ROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION,\n    sub_agents=[chat_agent],\n)\n```\n\nroot_agent instruction:\n- Never answer directly. Always delegate to chat_agent.\n- Domain: Bengaluru climate, flood, AQI, power, traffic only.\n- Reject non-Bengaluru geography queries before delegation.\n- Reject harmful / non-domain queries with a fixed refusal string.\n\n### 5.3 `subagents/chat_agent.py` — chat_agent\n\nThe only agent that synthesises a natural language response to the user.\n\nTools on chat_agent: `resolve_location`, `list_document_indexes`\n\nSub-agents: `flood_vulnerability_agent`, `heat_health_agent`, `infrastructure_agent`, `logistics_agent`\n\nInstruction responsibilities:\n1. **Input guardrail** — if the query is not about Bengaluru climate/infrastructure, respond with: `\"I can only answer questions about Bengaluru's climate, flood risk, air quality, power grid, and traffic. Please rephrase.\"`\n2. **Location resolution** — always call `resolve_location` before forwarding to domain agents. Canonical `location_id` must be established.\n3. **Routing** — map intents to agents:\n   - Flood / lake / inundation / drainage → `flood_vulnerability_agent`\n   - AQI / air quality / health / heat / UHI → `heat_health_agent`\n   - Power / outage / BESCOM / tree fall / wind → `infrastructure_agent`\n   - Traffic / corridor / ORR / delay / logistics → `logistics_agent`\n   - Multi-domain → delegate to multiple agents in sequence, then synthesise\n4. **Scorecard trigger** — if query contains `risk`, `scorecard`, `vulnerability`, `assessment`, `how vulnerable is` → call ALL four domain agents and synthesise the Unified Risk Scorecard format.\n5. **Citation rule** — every factual claim in the response must reference either a DB record timestamp or a document section ID. No unsourced numerical claims.\n6. **Data freshness** — include the observed_at timestamp of the most recent data point used in the response.\n\n### 5.4 `subagents/flood_agent.py` — flood_vulnerability_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — from `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_historical(location_id, hours)` — from `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n- `get_lake_hydrology(lake_id, limit)` — from `tools/flood_tools.py`\n- `get_flood_incidents(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/flood_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — from `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — from `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n\nInstruction:\n- Primary signal: `rainfall_intensity_mm_hr` from weather + `fill_percentage` + `overflow_status` from lake hydrology\n- Secondary signal: `flood_incident` history for the location (frequency, severity, water_depth_cm)\n- Document grounding: read flood study index → identify relevant sections (ones with `contains_thresholds: true` for the ward) → fetch section → extract threshold values → compare against live data\n- Barricade recommendation: only if `rainfall_intensity_mm_hr > threshold_from_document` AND (`fill_percentage > 85` OR `overflow_status = true`)\n- Output in structured format (see §7)\n- Known monitored lake IDs (from golden data): Bellandur, Varthur, KR Puram, Hebbal — map these from location resolution\n\n### 5.5 `subagents/heat_agent.py` — heat_health_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_aqi_current(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `get_aqi_historical(location_id, days)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared weather tool (temperature, humidity)\n- `get_ward_profile(ward_id)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- `aqi_value` + `aqi_category` + `dominant_pollutant` drive advisory specificity\n- `elderly_population_pct` + `children_population_pct` from ward_profile → sensitive population multiplier\n- `green_cover_pct` → UHI intensity factor\n- WHO thresholds applied per pollutant type (these are embedded in instruction, not from DB)\n- Requires specific advisory text per pollutant: PM2.5 → \"avoid outdoor exercise\", NO2 → \"keep windows closed\", etc.\n\n### 5.6 `subagents/infra_agent.py` — infrastructure_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared\n- `get_weather_historical(location_id, hours)` — shared (wind gust trend)\n- `get_power_outage_events(location_id, window_days)` — from `tools/infra_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- Primary signal: `wind_gust_kmh` (NOT `wind_speed_kmh`) from most recent weather observation\n- Only `outage_type != 'planned'` events are used for historical calibration\n- `fault_type = 'treefall'` events are the direct tree-fall risk signal\n- Document: BBMP tree canopy reference → tree density for the ward\n- Document: BESCOM infra reference → powerline proximity data for the ward\n- Risk tiers: `wind_gust_kmh < 40` = LOW, `40–60` = MODERATE, `60–80` = HIGH, `> 80` = CRITICAL (instruction-embedded, then calibrated by historical outage frequency)\n\n### 5.7 `subagents/logistics_agent.py` — logistics_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_traffic_current(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n- `get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name, limit)` — from `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared\n- `get_flood_incidents(location_id, limit)` — flood tools (waterlogging signals)\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- `congestion_index` + Session Persistence Flow\n\n```\nUser → POST /api/chat\n    │\n    ▼\nchat route (FastAPI)\n    │ ─── calls ──→ agent_proxy.get_chat_response(settings, db_session, session_id, message)\n    │                   │\n    │                   ├─ 1. _ensure_session (ADK InMemory)\n    │                   ├─ 2. POST /run → ADK → root_agent → chat_agent → domain agents\n    │                   ├─ 3. map ADK output to canonical Level-2 response contract\n    │                   ├─ 4. conversation_service.upsert_session(db_session, session_id)\n    │                   ├─ 5. conversation_service.append_turn(db_session, session_id, ...)\n    │                   └─ 6. await db_session.commit()\n    │\n    ▼\nLevel-2 response returned to user\n```\n\nThe DB write happens **after** the ADK call succeeds. If the DB write fails, the response is still returned to the user (log the error, don't fail the request). This is a best-effort persistence approach — data availability > perfect persistence.\n\n### 8.1 Canonical response contracts (Level-1 vs Level-2)\n\nLevel-1 is internal and mandatory for every domain agent call. Level-2 is the only backend response shape exposed to frontend clients.\n\n**Level-1 domain payload (internal)**\n- Strict typed schema with required fields: `agent`, `query_id`, `timestamp`, `status`, `confidence`, `data`, `citations`, `data_freshness`, `context_used`, `errors`\n- `status` enum is `ok | partial | error`\n- `context_used` is required to capture resolved location, intent, and operational assumptions\n- L1 payloads are not rendered directly in UI\n\n**Level-2 user delivery contract (backend -> frontend)**\n- Single stable shape for all chat responses:\n  - `session_id`\n  - `response_mode` (`text | scorecard | artifact`)\n  - `response_text` (always present)\n  - `citations_summary` (always present, may be empty)\n  - `data_freshness_summary` (always present)\n  - `risk_card` (nullable)\n  - `artifact` (nullable)\n  - `follow_up_prompt` (nullable)\n- `risk_card` and `artifact` are populated only when the user explicitly asks and the capability is implemented; otherwise they remain `null`\n- Mapping from Level-1 -> Level-2 is deterministic (no separate formatting agent)\n\nExample Level-2 response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid\",\n  \"response_mode\": \"text\",\n  \"response_text\": \"Bellandur flood risk is elevated tonight due to rainfall and lake fill conditions.\",\n  \"citations_summary\": [],\n  \"data_freshness_summary\": {\n    \"telemetry_lag_seconds\": 42,\n    \"stale_sources\": []\n  },\n  \"risk_card\": null,\n  \"artifact\": null,\n  \"follow_up_prompt\": null\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 9. Environment Variables\n\n### Backend additions (`server/.env`)\n\nNo new variables needed — the backend already has `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN`. The internal routes use it for verification.\n\n### Agents additions (`agents/.env`)\n\n```\nBACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://localhost:8010    # or http://server:8010 in Docker\nDOCUMENTS_PATH=/documents                     # mounted volume path\nAGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token       # same as backend\nAGENT_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash\nGOOGLE_API_KEY=<your_key>\n```\n\n### Docker Compose changes (agents service)\n\n```yaml\nagents:\n  ...\n  environment:\n    - BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://server:8010\n    - DOCUMENTS_PATH=/documents\n"
  }
}
```

**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:20:08.819Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_GVP9YikHM92lFW0w2QKiZSES",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 5cd829\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1843\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport time\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport httpx\nfrom loguru import logger\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\n\nfrom app.core.config import Settings\nfrom app.services import conversation_service\n\nCHAT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30.0\n\n\ndef _auth_headers(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n    token = settings.agent_internal_token.strip()\n    if not token:\n        raise ValueError(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for agent requests\")\n    return {\"X-Internal-Token\": token}\n\n\ndef _extract_message_from_events(events: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n    for event in reversed(events):\n        content = event.get(\"content\")\n        if not isinstance(content, dict):\n            continue\n        role = content.get(\"role\")\n        if role not in (None, \"model\"):\n            continue\n        parts = content.get(\"parts\")\n        if not isinstance(parts, list):\n            continue\n        texts = [\n            part.get(\"text\")\n            for part in parts\n            if isinstance(part, dict) and isinstance(part.get(\"text\"), str)\n        ]\n        if texts:\n            return \"\\n\".join(texts)\n    raise ValueError(\"Agent response did not include model text\")\n\n\nasync def _ensure_session(\n    client: httpx.AsyncClient,\n    base_url: str,\n    app_name: str,\n    user_id: str,\n    session_id: str,\n    headers: dict[str, str],\n) -> None:\n    response = await client.post(\n        f\"{base_url}/apps/{app_name}/users/{user_id}/sessions/{session_id}\",\n        headers=headers,\n        json=None,\n    )\n    if response.status_code in {200, 201, 204, 409}:\n        return\n    response.raise_for_status()\n\n\nasync def get_chat_response(\n    settings: Settings,\n    db_session: AsyncSession,\n    session_id: str,\n    message: str,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    started_at = time.perf_counter()\n    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=CHAT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) as client:\n        base_url = settings.agent_server_url.rstrip(\"/\")\n        app_name = settings.agent_app_name\n        user_id = \"metrosense\"\n        await _ensure_session(\n            client=client,\n            base_url=base_url,\n            app_name=app_name,\n            user_id=user_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            headers=headers,\n        )\n        response = await client.post(\n            f\"{base_url}/run\",\n            headers=headers,\n            json={\n                \"appName\": app_name,\n                \"userId\": user_id,\n                \"sessionId\": session_id,\n                \"newMessage\": {\"role\": \"user\", \"parts\": [{\"text\": message}]},\n                \"streaming\": False,\n            },\n        )\n        response.raise_for_status()\n        events = response.json()\n\n    if not isinstance(events, list):\n        raise ValueError(\"Agent response must be a list of events\")\n\n    message_text = _extract_message_from_events(events)\n    latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - started_at) * 1000)\n\n    try:\n        await conversation_service.upsert_session(db_session, session_id=session_id)\n        await conversation_service.append_turn(\n            db_session,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            user_message=message,\n            assistant_message=message_text,\n            agents_invoked=[],\n            latency_ms=latency_ms,\n        )\n        await db_session.commit()\n    except Exception:\n        await db_session.rollback()\n        logger.exception(\"Failed to persist conversation history for session_id={}\", session_id)\n\n    return {\n        \"message\": message_text,\n        \"risk_card\": None,\n        \"artifact\": None,\n    }\n\n\nasync def get_agent_health(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n        response = await client.get(\n            f\"{settings.agent_server_url.rstrip('/')}/health\",\n            headers=headers,\n        )\n        response.raise_for_status()\n\n    return {\"backend\": \"ok\", \"agent\": \"ok\", \"status\": \"online\"}\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport httpx\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, status\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\nfrom starlette.responses import JSONResponse\n\nfrom app.api.deps import db_session, settings\nfrom app.core.config import Settings\nfrom app.services import agent_proxy\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/api\", tags=[\"chat\"])\n\n\nclass ArtifactPayload(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    type: str\n    title: str\n    source: str\n    description: str | None = None\n\n\nclass RiskMetric(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    probability: float | None = None\n    severity: str | None = None\n    congestion_score: float | None = None\n\n\nclass HealthAdvisory(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    aqi: int | None = None\n    aqi_category: str | None = None\n\n\nclass EmergencyReadiness(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    recommendation: str\n    actions: list[str] | None = None\n\n\nclass RiskCardPayload(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    neighborhood: str\n    generated_at: str\n    overall_risk_score: float\n    flood_risk: RiskMetric | None = None\n    power_outage_risk: RiskMetric | None = None\n    traffic_delay_index: RiskMetric | None = None\n    health_advisory: HealthAdvisory | None = None\n    emergency_readiness: EmergencyReadiness | None = None\n\n\nclass ChatRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    message: str\n\n\nclass ChatResponse(BaseModel):\n    message: str\n    risk_card: RiskCardPayload | None = None\n    artifact: ArtifactPayload | None = None\n\n\nclass ErrorPayload(BaseModel):\n    code: str\n    message: str\n\n\n\n\ndef _error_response(code: str, message: str, status_code: int) -> JSONResponse:\n    return JSONResponse(\n        status_code=status_code,\n        content={\"error\": ErrorPayload(code=code, message=message).model_dump()},\n    )\n\n\n@router.post(\n    \"/chat\",\n    response_model=ChatResponse,\n    responses={\n        502: {\"description\": \"Agent unavailable\"},\n        504: {\"description\": \"Agent timeout\"},\n    },\n)\nasync def chat(\n    payload: ChatRequest,\n    app_settings: Settings = Depends(settings),\n    session: AsyncSession = Depends(db_session),\n) -> ChatResponse | JSONResponse:\n    try:\n        response_payload = await agent_proxy.get_chat_response(\n            settings=app_settings,\n            db_session=session,\n            session_id=payload.session_id,\n            message=payload.message,\n        )\n    except httpx.TimeoutException:\n        return _error_response(\n            code=\"AGENT_TIMEOUT\",\n            message=\"The agent service did not respond in time.\",\n            status_code=status.HTTP_504_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT,\n        )\n    except (httpx.HTTPError, ValueError):\n        return _error_response(\n            code=\"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\",\n            message=\"The agent service is currently unavailable.\",\n            status_code=status.HTTP_502_BAD_GATEWAY,\n        )\n\n    return ChatResponse.model_validate(response_payload)\n\n\n@router.delete(\"/chat/{session_id}\", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)\nasync def clear_chat_session(\n    session_id: str,  # noqa: ARG001 - placeholder until agents service supports reset\n    app_settings: Settings = Depends(settings),  # noqa: ARG001\n) -> None:\n    return None\n"
  }
}

  }
}

## Entry 449
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:20:27.423Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:20:27.423Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "request_user_input",
    "arguments": "{\"questions\":[{\"header\":\"Tool contract\",\"id\":\"phase4_tool_contract\",\"question\":\"For Phase 4 agent prompts/tool usage, which tool return format should be canonical?\",\"options\":[{\"label\":\"Keep envelope (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Use current `{data, meta}` shape and teach agents to read `data` while using `meta` for degradation/caveats.\"},{\"label\":\"Revert to raw data\",\"description\":\"Tools return list/dict only; no metadata in function output.\"},{\"label\":\"Hybrid by tool\",\"description\":\"Some tools return envelope, others raw data depending on domain.\"}]},{\"header\":\"Agent identity\",\"id\":\"phase4_identity\",\"question\":\"Which ADK app/root naming should we lock for Phase 4?\",\"options\":[{\"label\":\"metrosense_agent + root_agent (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Set backend `agent_app_name=metrosense_agent`; root agent object is `root_agent`.\"},{\"label\":\"Keep metrosearch_agent\",\"description\":\"Continue using legacy app name for compatibility this phase.\"},{\"label\":\"Dual registration\",\"description\":\"Expose both app names temporarily and migrate later.\"}]},{\"header\":\"L2 integration\",\"id\":\"phase4_l2_integration\",\"question\":\"Should Phase 4 include backend parsing to canonical Level-2 response now, or defer to Phase 5?\",\"options\":[{\"label\":\"Include now (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Update `agent_proxy`/chat route in Phase 4 so ADK output is mapped to canonical L2 immediately.\"},{\"label\":\"Defer parsing\",\"description\":\"Keep `message`-only response now and add canonical L2 mapping in Phase 5.\"},{\"label\":\"Partial now\",\"description\":\"Keep message response but add placeholder fields without robust mapping logic.\"}]}]}",
    "call_id": "call_dk8T0rKfGQVXkwrzX6Xe4mVL"
  }
}
```

## Entry 453
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:27:12.545Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:27:12.545Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Noted. One important clarification: “Hybrid by tool” can be done, but we should lock exactly which tools keep envelope vs raw to avoid prompt/runtime bugs. Also, there is no explicit “Google ADK skill” available in this workspace; I’m basing the plan on `AGENTS_ADK.md` + current ADK package patterns in-repo. I need one final mapping decision.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Noted. One important clarification: “Hybrid by tool” can be done, but we should lock exactly which tools keep envelope vs raw to avoid prompt/runtime bugs. Also, there is no explicit “Google ADK skill” available in this workspace; I’m basing the plan on `AGENTS_ADK.md` + current ADK package patterns in-repo. I need one final mapping decision.",
    "phase": "commentary"
  }
}
```

## Entry 459
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:33:24.053Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:33:24.053Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cdc34-c8e6-7c01-95f0-9429ce05345c",
    "turn_id": "019cdc68-f430-7e10-aa28-4041dbd6dbd0",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cdc68-f430-7e10-aa28-4041dbd6dbd0-plan",
      "text": "## Phase 4 Plan: ADK Agent Orchestration, Guardrails, and Session-Aware Execution\n\n### Summary\nImplement Phase 4 by replacing the current `metrosense_agent` placeholders with a full ADK hierarchy:\n- `root_agent` (orchestrator, non-conversational)\n- `chat_agent` (only user-facing agent)\n- four domain sub-agents (flood, heat, infra, logistics)\n\nLock runtime identity to `agent_app_name=metrosense_agent` and integrate canonical Level-2 response mapping in backend now.  \nUse hybrid tool returns as decided: backend tools use `{data, meta}`, document tools return raw data.\n\n### Key Implementation Changes\n- Build ADK hierarchy and wiring:\n  - `root_agent` with `sub_agents=[chat_agent]`, strict delegation-only instruction.\n  - `chat_agent` with domain sub-agents + shared tools (`resolve_location`, document index lookup) and full guardrails.\n  - Domain agents registered under chat agent, each with only its domain toolset + shared weather/docs where required.\n- Curate prompt/instruction contracts in `prompts.py`:\n  - Root: routing only, no direct user answer, domain boundary enforcement.\n  - Chat: off-domain refusal, injection handling, location resolution required before domain calls, scorecard trigger keywords, citation/freshness requirements.\n  - Domain agents: deterministic reasoning instructions per domain, required fields for Level-1 payload.\n- Define state/session handling behavior:\n  - Root and chat consume `session_id` + provided history from backend call context.\n  - Chat maintains rolling session context (last N turns from backend history payload), not full history replay.\n  - Multi-domain queries dispatch in parallel and aggregate as partial/ok/error with surfaced caveats.\n- Align backend runtime integration:\n  - Update backend config to `agent_app_name=\"metrosense_agent\"`.\n  - Update `agent_proxy` parsing: extract Level-2 fields (response text + nullable structured blocks + freshness/citation summaries), not message-only payload.\n  - Keep DB persistence flow unchanged (best-effort write-through after ADK response).\n- Tool contract application (locked):\n  - Backend tools: `{data, meta}` and agent logic must read `meta.ok/error_code` for degraded responses.\n  - Document tools: raw index/section data for simpler prompt parsing.\n  - Chat/domain prompts must explicitly describe this mixed shape to avoid misuse.\n\n### Interfaces and Contracts\n- Agent app identity:\n  - ADK app: `metrosense_agent`\n  - root symbol exported: `root_agent`\n- Level-1 internal payload required from each domain agent:\n  - `agent, query_id, timestamp, status, confidence, data, citations, data_freshness, context_used, errors`\n- Level-2 backend response required now:\n  - `session_id, response_mode, response_text, citations_summary, data_freshness_summary, risk_card, artifact, follow_up_prompt`\n- Guardrail constants (single source in prompts):\n  - off-domain refusal\n  - location-not-found response\n  - no-data/degraded-data response using backend tool `meta`\n\n### Test Plan\n- Unit tests (agents package):\n  - prompt/instruction conformance smoke (agent objects instantiate with expected sub-agent graph).\n  - routing tests for chat agent intent-to-domain mapping (single/multi-domain/scorecard trigger/off-domain).\n  - degradation tests: backend tool `meta.ok=false` leads to `partial` status and explicit caveat text.\n- Backend integration tests:\n  - `/api/chat` with ADK response now returns canonical Level-2 shape, not message-only.\n  - `agent_app_name=metrosense_agent` path works end-to-end through proxy.\n  - persistence still writes `sessions` and `conversation_history` after ADK success.\n- Runtime acceptance scenarios:\n  - flood-only query\n  - mixed flood+logistics query (parallel fan-out)\n  - scorecard-trigger query\n  - location resolution failure\n  - backend tool timeout/unauthorized fallback\n  - document section not found fallback\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Google ADK “skill” is not available in this workspace; implementation uses repository ADK guidance (`AGENTS_ADK.md`) and current ADK package patterns.\n- Phase 3 tools are available and remain source-of-truth for function names.\n- Structured blocks (`risk_card`, `artifact`) remain nullable unless explicitly requested and produced by current capability.\n- No SSE/streaming changes in this phase; synchronous request/response behavior is preserved.\n"
    }
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**

`/api/chat` with ADK response now returns canonical Level-2 shape, not message-only.\n  - `agent_app_name=metrosense_agent` path works end-to-end through proxy.\n  - persistence still writes `sessions` and `conversation_history` after ADK success.\n- Runtime acceptance scenarios:\n  - flood-only query\n  - mixed flood+logistics query (parallel fan-out)\n  - scorecard-trigger query\n  - location resolution failure\n  - backend tool timeout/unauthorized fallback\n  - document section not found fallback\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Google ADK “skill” is not available in this workspace; implementation uses repository ADK guidance (`AGENTS_ADK.md`) and current ADK package patterns.\n- Phase 3 tools are available and remain source-of-truth for function names.\n- Structured blocks (`risk_card`, `artifact`) remain nullable unless explicitly requested and produced by current capability.\n- No SSE/streaming changes in this phase; synchronous request/response behavior is preserved.\n"
    }
  }
}

location-not-found response\n  - no-data/degraded-data response using backend tool `meta`\n\n### Test Plan\n- Unit tests (agents package):\n  - prompt/instruction conformance smoke (agent objects instantiate with expected sub-agent graph).\n  - routing tests for chat agent intent-to-domain mapping (single/multi-domain/scorecard trigger/off-domain).\n  - degradation tests: backend tool `meta.ok=false` leads to `partial` status and explicit caveat text.\n- Backend integration tests:\n  - `/api/chat` with ADK response now returns canonical Level-2 shape, not message-only.\n  - `agent_app_name=metrosense_agent` path works end-to-end through proxy.\n  - persistence still writes `sessions` and `conversation_history` after ADK success.\n- Runtime acceptance scenarios:\n  - flood-only query\n  - mixed flood+logistics query (parallel fan-out)\n  - scorecard-trigger query\n  - location resolution failure\n  - backend tool timeout/unauthorized fallback\n  - document section not found fallback\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Google ADK “skill” is not available in this workspace; implementation uses repository ADK guidance (`AGENTS_ADK.md`) and current ADK package patterns.\n- Phase 3 tools are available and remain source-of-truth for function names.\n- Structured blocks (`risk_card`, `artifact`) remain nullable unless explicitly requested and produced by current capability.\n- No SSE/streaming changes in this phase; synchronous request/response behavior is preserved.\n</proposed_plan>"
      }
    ],
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

`root_agent` with `sub_agents=[chat_agent]`, strict delegation-only instruction.\n  - `chat_agent` with domain sub-agents + shared tools (`resolve_location`, document index lookup) and full guardrails.\n  - Domain agents registered under chat agent, each with only its domain toolset + shared weather/docs where required.\n- Curate prompt/instruction contracts in `prompts.py`:\n  - Root: routing only, no direct user answer, domain boundary enforcement.\n  - Chat: off-domain refusal, injection handling, location resolution required before domain calls, scorecard trigger keywords, citation/freshness requirements.\n  - Domain agents: deterministic reasoning instructions per domain, required fields for Level-1 payload.\n- Define state/session handling behavior:\n  - Root and chat consume `session_id` + provided history from backend call context.\n  - Chat maintains rolling session context (last N turns from backend history payload), not full history replay.\n  - Multi-domain queries dispatch in parallel and aggregate as partial/ok/error with surfaced caveats.\n- Align backend runtime integration:\n  - Update backend config to `agent_app_name=\"metrosense_agent\"`.\n  - Update `agent_proxy` parsing: extract Level-2 fields (response text + nullable structured blocks + freshness/citation summaries), not message-only payload.\n  - Keep DB persistence flow unchanged (best-effort write-through after ADK response).\n- Tool contract application (locked):\n  - Backend tools: `{data, meta}` and agent logic must read `meta.ok/error_code` for degraded responses.\n  - Document tools: raw index/section data for simpler prompt parsing.\n  - Chat/domain prompts must explicitly describe this mixed shape to avoid misuse.\n\n### Interfaces and Contracts\n- Agent app identity:\n  - ADK app: `metrosense_agent`\n  - root symbol exported: `root_agent`\n- Level-1 internal payload required from each domain agent:\n  - `agent, query_id, timestamp, status, confidence, data, citations, data_freshness, context_used, errors`\n- Level-2 backend response required now:\n  - `session_id, response_mode, response_text, citations_summary, data_freshness_summary, risk_card, artifact, follow_up_prompt`\n- Guardrail constants (single source in prompts):\n  - off-domain refusal\n  - routing tests for chat agent intent-to-domain mapping (single/multi-domain/scorecard trigger/off-domain).\n  - degradation tests: backend tool `meta.ok=false` leads to `partial` status and explicit caveat text.\n- Backend integration tests:\n  - `/api/chat` with ADK response now returns canonical Level-2 shape, not message-only.\n  - `agent_app_name=metrosense_agent` path works end-to-end through proxy.\n  - persistence still writes `sessions` and `conversation_history` after ADK success.\n- Runtime acceptance scenarios:\n  - flood-only query\n  - mixed flood+logistics query (parallel fan-out)\n  - scorecard-trigger query\n  - location resolution failure\n  - backend tool timeout/unauthorized fallback\n  - document section not found fallback\n\n### Assumptions and Defaults\n- Google ADK “skill” is not available in this workspace; implementation uses repository ADK guidance (`AGENTS_ADK.md`) and current ADK package patterns.\n- Phase 3 tools are available and remain source-of-truth for function names.\n- Structured blocks (`risk_card`, `artifact`) remain nullable unless explicitly requested and produced by current capability.\n- No SSE/streaming changes in this phase; synchronous request/response behavior is preserved.\n</proposed_plan>"

## Entry 464
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:33:58.676Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:33:58.676Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc75-e092-7961-874b-92a2da672ee9",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "plan",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only, concise by default, and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nWhen possible, prefer a compact structure with 3-5 short sections, usually: Summary, Key Changes or Implementation Changes, Test Plan, and Assumptions. Do not include a separate Scope section unless scope boundaries are genuinely important to avoid mistakes.\n\nPrefer grouped implementation bullets by subsystem or behavior over file-by-file inventories. Mention files only when needed to disambiguate a non-obvious change, and avoid naming more than 3 paths unless extra specificity is necessary to prevent mistakes. Prefer behavior-level descriptions over symbol-by-symbol removal lists. For v1 feature-addition plans, do not invent detailed schema, validation, precedence, fallback, or wire-shape policy unless the request establishes it or it is needed to prevent a concrete implementation mistake; prefer the intended capability and minimum interface/behavior changes.\n\nKeep bullets short and avoid explanatory sub-bullets unless they are needed to prevent ambiguity. Prefer the minimum detail needed for implementation safety, not exhaustive coverage. Within each section, compress related changes into a few high-signal bullets and omit branch-by-branch logic, repeated invariants, and long lists of unaffected behavior unless they are necessary to prevent a likely implementation mistake. Avoid repeated repo facts and irrelevant edge-case or rollout detail. For straightforward refactors, keep the plan to a compact summary, key edits, tests, and assumptions. If the user asks for more detail, then expand.\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities.
