{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:40:09.074Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_fo1MOHxLlNPQlIAAZ9nuvZTb",
    "output": "Chunk ID: a02a9d\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1752\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport pytest\nfrom httpx import AsyncClient\n\nfrom app.api.deps import require_user\nfrom app.db.models import User\nfrom app.main import app\nfrom app.services import agent_proxy\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef auth_override() -> None:\n    app.dependency_overrides[require_user] = lambda: User(\n        id=1,\n        email=\"test@example.com\",\n        password_hash=\"x\",\n    )\n    yield\n    app.dependency_overrides.pop(require_user, None)\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_chat_returns_json_payload(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    async def _fake_chat_response(**_: object) -> dict[str, object]:\n        return {\n            \"message\": \"Flood risk is elevated tonight.\",\n            \"risk_card\": {\n                \"neighborhood\": \"SARJAPUR ROAD\",\n                \"generated_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00Z\",\n                \"overall_risk_score\": 8.8,\n            },\n            \"artifact\": {\n                \"type\": \"html\",\n                \"title\": \"Rainfall Trend\",\n                \"source\": \"<div>chart</div>\",\n            },\n        }\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy, \"get_chat_response\", _fake_chat_response)\n\n    response = await client.post(\n        \"/api/chat\",\n        json={\n            \"session_id\": \"test-session\",\n            \"message\": \"What is the flood risk tonight?\",\n        },\n    )\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"message\": \"Flood risk is elevated tonight.\",\n        \"risk_card\": {\n            \"neighborhood\": \"SARJAPUR ROAD\",\n            \"generated_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00Z\",\n            \"overall_risk_score\": 8.8,\n            \"flood_risk\": None,\n            \"power_outage_risk\": None,\n            \"traffic_delay_index\": None,\n            \"health_advisory\": None,\n            \"emergency_readiness\": None,\n        },\n        \"artifact\": {\n            \"type\": \"html\",\n            \"title\": \"Rainfall Trend\",\n            \"source\": \"<div>chart</div>\",\n            \"description\": None,\n        },\n    }\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_chat_returns_agent_error_payload(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    async def _raise_error(**_: object) -> dict[str, object]:\n        raise ValueError(\"bad payload\")\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy, \"get_chat_response\", _raise_error)\n\n    response = await client.post(\n        \"/api/chat\",\n        json={\"session_id\": \"test-session\", \"message\": \"hello\"},\n    )\n\n    assert response.status_code == 502\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"error\": {\n            \"code\": \"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\",\n            \"message\": \"The agent service is currently unavailable.\",\n        }\n    }\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_api_health_degraded_when_agent_is_down(\n    client: AsyncClient, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, auth_override: None\n) -> None:\n    async def _fake_health(_: object) -> dict[str, str]:\n        return {\"backend\": \"ok\", \"agent\": \"down\", \"status\": \"degraded\"}\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy, \"get_agent_health\", _fake_health)\n\n    response = await client.get(\"/api/health\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\n        \"backend\": \"ok\",\n        \"agent\": \"down\",\n        \"status\": \"degraded\",\n    }\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing import Any\nfrom unittest.mock import AsyncMock\n\nimport httpx\nimport pytest\n\nfrom app.core.config import Settings\nfrom app.services import agent_proxy\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_agent_proxy_requires_internal_token() -> None:\n    settings = Settings(agent_internal_token=\"\")\n    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\"):\n        await agent_proxy.get_agent_health(settings)\n\n\nclass _FakeResponse:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        status_code: int = 200,\n        json_payload: Any = None,\n        should_raise: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.status_code = status_code\n        self._json_payload = json_payload\n        self._should_raise = should_raise\n\n    def json(self) -> Any:\n        return self._json_payload\n\n    def raise_for_status(self) -> None:\n        if self._should_raise:\n            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError(\n                \"error\",\n                request=httpx.Request(\"POST\", \"http://test/run\"),\n                response=httpx.Response(500),\n            )\n\n\nclass _FakeAsyncClient:\n    def __init__(self, *_: object, **__: object) -> None:\n        self._call_count = 0\n\n    async def __aenter__(self) -> _FakeAsyncClient:\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type: object, exc: object, tb: object) -> None:\n        return None\n\n    async def post(self, *_: object, **__: object) -> _FakeResponse:\n        self._call_count += 1\n        if self._call_count == 1:\n            return _FakeResponse(status_code=201)\n        return _FakeResponse(\n            json_payload=[\n                {\"content\": {\"role\": \"model\", \"parts\": [{\"text\": \"Persisted assistant reply\"}]}}\n            ]\n        )\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_get_chat_response_persists_and_commits(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:\n    settings = Settings(\n        agent_internal_token=\"test-token\",\n        agent_server_url=\"http://agent.local\",\n    )\n    db_session = AsyncMock()\n    upsert = AsyncMock()\n    append = AsyncMock(return_value=\"turn-id\")\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy.httpx, \"AsyncClient\", _FakeAsyncClient)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy.conversation_service, \"upsert_session\", upsert)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy.conversation_service, \"append_turn\", append)\n\n    payload = await agent_proxy.get_chat_response(\n        settings=settings,\n        db_session=db_session,\n        session_id=\"s-1\",\n        message=\"hello\",\n    )\n\n    assert payload[\"message\"] == \"Persisted assistant reply\"\n    upsert.assert_awaited_once()\n    append.assert_awaited_once()\n    db_session.commit.assert_awaited_once()\n    db_session.rollback.assert_not_awaited()\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_get_chat_response_returns_when_persistence_fails(\n    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,\n) -> None:\n    settings = Settings(\n        agent_internal_token=\"test-token\",\n        agent_server_url=\"http://agent.local\",\n    )\n    db_session = AsyncMock()\n    upsert = AsyncMock()\n    append = AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError(\"db write failure\"))\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy.httpx, \"AsyncClient\", _FakeAsyncClient)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy.conversation_service, \"upsert_session\", upsert)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(agent_proxy.conversation_service, \"append_turn\", append)\n\n    payload = await agent_proxy.get_chat_response(\n        settings=settings,\n        db_session=db_session,\n        session_id=\"s-2\",\n        message=\"hello\",\n    )\n\n    assert payload[\"message\"] == \"Persisted assistant reply\"\n    upsert.assert_awaited_once()\n    append.assert_awaited_once()\n    db_session.rollback.assert_awaited_once()\n    db_session.commit.assert_not_awaited()\n"
  }
}

 session_id=\"s-2\",\n        message=\"hello\",\n    )\n\n    assert payload[\"message\"] == \"Persisted assistant reply\"\n    upsert.assert_awaited_once()\n    append.assert_awaited_once()\n    db_session.rollback.assert_awaited_once()\n    db_session.commit.assert_not_awaited()\n"
  }
}
```

AsyncClient\nfrom sqlalchemy import select\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\n\nfrom app.db.models import User\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_auth_chat_logout_flow(client: AsyncClient, db: AsyncSession) -> None:\n    email = \"integration@example.com\"\n    password = \"password123\"\n\n    signup = await client.post(\"/api/auth/signup\", json={\"email\": email, \"password\": password})\n    assert signup.status_code == 201\n\n    result = await db.execute(select(User).where(User.email == email))\n    assert result.scalar_one_or_none() is not None\n\n    chat = await client.post(\"/api/chat\", json={\"session_id\": \"integration\", \"message\": \"hello\"})\n    assert chat.status_code == 200\n    assert chat.json()[\"message\"] == \"Stubbed response from agent.\"\n\n    logout = await client.post(\"/api/auth/logout\")\n    assert logout.status_code == 204\n\n    chat_after_logout = await client.post(\n        \"/api/chat\", json={\"session_id\": \"integration\", \"message\": \"hello again\"}\n    )\n    assert chat_after_logout.status_code == 401\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_login_flow(client: AsyncClient) -> None:\n    email = \"loginflow@example.com\"\n    password = \"password123\"\n\n    await client.post(\"/api/auth/signup\", json={\"email\": email, \"password\": password})\n    login = await client.post(\"/api/auth/login\", json={\"email\": email, \"password\": password})\n    assert login.status_code == 200\n    assert login.json()[\"user\"][\"email\"] == email\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport pytest\nfrom httpx import AsyncClient\nfrom sqlalchemy import func, select\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\n\nfrom app.db.models import ConversationHistory, Session\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_chat_persists_session_and_turn_history(\n    client: AsyncClient, db: AsyncSession\n) -> None:\n    email = \"persist@example.com\"\n    password = \"password123\"\n    session_id = \"persist-session\"\n\n    signup = await client.post(\"/api/auth/signup\", json={\"email\": email, \"password\": password})\n    assert signup.status_code == 201\n\n    first_chat = await client.post(\n        \"/api/chat\",\n        json={\"session_id\": session_id, \"message\": \"hello first turn\"},\n    )\n    assert first_chat.status_code == 200\n\n    second_chat = await client.post(\n        \"/api/chat\",\n        json={\"session_id\": session_id, \"message\": \"hello second turn\"},\n    )\n    assert second_chat.status_code == 200\n\n    persisted_session = (\n        await db.execute(select(Session).where(Session.session_id == session_id))\n    ).scalar_one()\n    assert persisted_session.total_turns == 2\n    assert persisted_session.active_flag is True\n\n    row_count = (\n        await db.execute(\n            select(func.count()).select_from(\n                ConversationHistory,\n            ).where(ConversationHistory.session_id == session_id)\n        )\n    ).scalar_one()\n    assert row_count == 4\n\n    rows = (\n        await db.execute(\n            select(ConversationHistory)\n            .where(ConversationHistory.session_id == session_id)\n            .order_by(ConversationHistory.timestamp.asc())\n        )\n    ).scalars().all()\n\n    user_rows = [row for row in rows if row.role == \"user\"]\n    assistant_rows = [row for row in rows if row.role == \"assistant\"]\n\n    assert len(user_rows) == 2\n    assert len(assistant_rows) == 2\n    assert {row.message for row in user_rows} == {\"hello first turn\", \"hello second turn\"}\n    assert all(row.message == \"Stubbed response from agent.\" for row in assistant_rows)\n    assert all(row.latency_ms is not None for row in assistant_rows)\n"
  }
}

{

  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents && uv run python - <<'PY'\\nfrom metrosense_agent.agent import root_agent\\nfrom metrosense_agent.subagents import chat_agent\\nfrom metrosense_agent.orchestration.routing import classify_intents, determine_response_mode\\n\\nprint(root_agent.name)\\nprint(chat_agent.name)\\nintent = classify_intents('Flood and traffic risk in Bellandur tonight')\\nprint(intent.flood, intent.logistics)\\nprint(determine_response_mode('Give risk scorecard for Bellandur'))\\nPY\",\"sandbox_permissions\":\"require_escalated\",\"justification\":\"Do you want me to run a quick uv sanity check to verify the new agents graph and routing helpers import correctly?\",\"prefix_rule\":[\"uv\",\"run\"],\"max_output_tokens\":12000}",
    "call_id": "call_PwxYJ6xWeCyCG5GOLpvjqj1W"
  }
}
```

{

  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Implemented Phase 4 end-to-end with proper agent structure, config/prompt separation, ADK hierarchy, and backend integration.\n\n**What I changed**\n\n- Built the new `metrosense_agent` structure with strict separation:\n  - config: [config.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/config.py)\n  - prompts package: [prompts/__init__.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/prompts/__init__.py)\n  - orchestration helpers: [orchestration/routing.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/orchestration/routing.py), [orchestration/response_contract.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/orchestration/response_contract.py)\n  - subagents: [subagents/chat_agent.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/chat_agent.py), [subagents/flood_agent.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/flood_agent.py), [subagents/heat_agent.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/heat_agent.py), [subagents/infra_agent.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/infra_agent.py), [subagents/logistics_agent.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/subagents/logistics_agent.py)\n  - root export: [agent.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/agent.py), [__init__.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/__init__.py)\n\n- Implemented ADK hierarchy exactly:\n  - `root_agent -> chat_agent -> [flood_vulnerability_agent, heat_health_agent, infrastructure_agent, logistics_agent]`.\n\n- Applied your hybrid tool contract decision:\n  - backend tools keep `{data, meta}` envelope.\n  - document tools now return raw values (`list` / `dict | None`) in [document_tools.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tools/shared/document_tools.py).\n\n- Backend integration updates:\n  - switched app name to metrosense: [server/app/core/config.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/core/config.py)\n  - `agent_proxy` now maps ADK output to canonical Level-2 fields with compatibility fallback: [server/app/services/agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/services/agent_proxy.py)\n  - `/api/chat` response model updated to canonical fields while keeping `message` backward-compatible: [server/app/api/routes/chat.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/app/api/routes/chat.py)\n\n- Added/updated tests for Phase 4 behavior:\n  - new agent/orchestration tests under [agents/metrosense_agent/tests](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tests)\n  - updated server unit tests for new response shape:\n    - [server/tests/unit/test_chat_api.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_chat_api.py)\n    - [server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py)\n\n**Validation run**\n\n- Passed: `server` unit tests\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_chat_api.py tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py -q` (6 passed)\n- Passed: agent compile + runtime sanity import\n  - `python3 -m compileall metrosense_agent`\n  - `uv run python` sanity confirmed root/chat/domain subagent wiring.\n- Not run: full `agents` pytest suite (`pytest` is not installed in the agents env yet).\n\nIf you want, next I can wire Phase 4 smoke tests through actual ADK `/run` calls (flood query, health query, scorecard trigger) against your running backend/proxy setup.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

new agent/orchestration tests under [agents/metrosense_agent/tests](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents/metrosense_agent/tests)\n  - updated server unit tests for new response shape:\n    - [server/tests/unit/test_chat_api.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_chat_api.py)\n    - [server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py](/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/server/tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py)\n\n**Validation run**\n\n- Passed: `server` unit tests\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_chat_api.py tests/unit/test_agent_proxy.py -q` (6 passed)\n- Passed: agent compile + runtime sanity import\n  - `python3 -m compileall metrosense_agent`\n  - `uv run python` sanity confirmed root/chat/domain subagent wiring.\n- Not run: full `agents` pytest suite (`pytest` is not installed in the agents env yet).\n\nIf you want, next I can wire Phase 4 smoke tests through actual ADK `/run` calls (flood query, health query, scorecard trigger) against your running backend/proxy setup."
  }
}
```

## Entry 619
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:41:55.129Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:41:55.129Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc7d-25b7-70a0-928a-3cb85b8e285f",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

`uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

## Entry 664
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:43:00.190Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:43:00.190Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc7e-23dc-7080-a0d0-47f1551f3203",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. ## Entry 682
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:43:41.692Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:43:41.692Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc7e-c5fa-7db0-a888-cd7e35f0896d",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}

## Entry 690
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T10:43:58.098Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:43:58.098Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Run it like this.\n\n1. Start DB + backend (tools depend on backend `/internal/*`)\n```bash\ncd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense\ndocker compose up -d db\n\ncd server\nuv sync --all-extras\nexport AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token\nuv run alembic upgrade head\nuv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010\n```\n\n2. Start ADK API server (new app is `metrosense_agent`)\n```bash\ncd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents\nuv sync\nexport AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token\nexport BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8010\nexport DOCUMENTS_PATH=../server/Documents_Metrosense\nuv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .\n```\n\n3. Start proxy (backend calls this)\n```bash\ncd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents\nexport AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token\nexport ADK_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8021\nuv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020\n```\n\n4. Open ADK Web UI for manual agent testing\n```bash\ncd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/agents\nuv run adk web --port 8001\n```\nOpen: `http://127.0.0.1:8001`\n\n5. Quick checks\n- Proxy health: `curl -i http://127.0.0.1:8020/health`\n- Backend health: `curl -i http://127.0.0.1:8010/api/health`\n- If backend chat says app not found, confirm backend is using `agent_app_name=metrosense_agent` (already default in code now).\n\nIf you want, I can give you a 5-query checklist to validate flood/heat/infra/logistics + scorecard paths in ADK Web.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```
