```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T10:51:41.299Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc86-1771-7861-b7ff-468ae839e766",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 796
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T11:03:30.148Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T11:03:30.148Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc90-e862-7ee2-b257-a4615c898630",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  -         "type": "input_text",
```
```json
        "text": "Here's an assessment of flood risk in Bellandur, based on available documentation. Please note that I was unable to retrieve real-time current weather or lake hydrology data due to a backend connectivity issue. Therefore, this assessment focuses on the established flood vulnerability patterns and thresholds for Bellandur.\n\nBellandur is highly susceptible to flooding primarily due to a compound flood mechanism: a combination of direct rainfall (pluvial) and backflow from Bellandur lake. This means that even moderate rainfall can lead to severe inundation, especially when Bellandur lake levels are high.\n\nHigh-Risk Locations in Bellandur:\n\nBellandur ORR Underpass: This is identified as a primary failure point due to its undersized box culvert and critically insufficient sump pump capacity. It is also directly impacted by Bellandur lake backflow.\nAgara Junction: A secondary failure point that typically experiences closure 15–20 minutes after the Bellandur ORR underpass.\nBellandur Main Road (near State Bank Colony): This low-lying stretch is prone to surface flooding, with typical depths of 30–45 cm during severe events.\nRainfall and Lake Level Thresholds for Inundation (for sustained rainfall for at least 20 minutes):\n\nThe likelihood and severity of flooding are significantly influenced by Bellandur lake's fill level. Here are the thresholds:\n\nSeverity\tBellandur Lake ≤ 60% Fill\tBellandur Lake ≥ 70% Fill (Watch)\tBellandur Lake ≥ 82% Fill (Warning)\tBellandur Lake ≥ 92% Fill (Danger)\nT1 (Inundation Onset)\t18 mm/hr\t14 mm/hr\t10 mm/hr\t6 mm/hr\nT2 (Traffic Affected)\t28 mm/hr\t22 mm/hr\t16 mm/hr\t10 mm/hr\nT3 (Closure Likely)\t38 mm/hr\t30 mm/hr\t22 mm/hr\t14 mm/hr\nImportant Considerations:\n\nLake Backflow Dominance: When Bellandur lake is above 94% fill, lake backflow is the primary driver of inundation, contributing 71–89% of observed flood depths. In such conditions, moderate sustained rainfall (e.g., 20 mm/hr for 60 minutes) can be more dangerous than intense but brief downpours.\nHysteresis Effect: Even after rainfall stops and lake levels begin to decline, the risk of backflow and inundation persists for 4–8 hours, impeding drainage from underpass sumps.\nAntecedent Rainfall: Prior rainfall increases flood risk. If 24-hour prior rainfall exceeds 50 mm, all thresholds are reduced by 15%. If 72-hour prior rainfall exceeds 120 mm, all thresholds are reduced by 25%.\nEarly Warning and Barricade Triggers:\n\nTo mitigate flood impact, specific triggers are recommended:\n\nBellandur ORR Underpass Barricade Trigger:\nRainfall intensity ≥ 32 mm/hr sustained for 20+ minutes, OR\nBellandur lake fill ≥ 85% AND rainfall ≥ 18 mm/hr, OR\nSump pump failure alarm is active.\nTime to road closure from trigger: 25–40 minutes.\nPre-emptive Barricading: If Bellandur lake fill exceeds 95%, pre-emptive barricading of the Bellandur ORR underpass and Agara underpass is recommended, regardless of current rainfall.\nIn summary, Bellandur's flood risk tonight would be determined by a combination of actual rainfall intensity, the current fill level of Bellandur lake, and any recent heavy rainfall. Without real-time data, it's not possible to provide a specific forecast for tonight. However, the documented thresholds highlight that even moderate rainfall can cause significant disruption if the lake is at high fill levels.\n\nConfidence: Medium (Due to lack of real-time data, but comprehensive understanding of vulnerability from documents). Data Freshness: Documents published between 2019 and 2022. Context Used:\n\nbangalore_flood_study_2022 (sections s2, s3, s5)\ndrainage_plan_bbmp_2019 (sections s2, s5) Citations:\nUrban Flood Vulnerability and Inundation Dynamics in Bangalore — A Technical Study (Indian Institute of Science (IISc) — Centre for Infrastructure, Sustainable Transportation and Urban Planning (CiSTUP), November 2022)\nBBMP Stormwater Drainage Master Plan — Bengaluru (Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP), June 2019) Errors: Backend HTTP errors were encountered when attempting to retrieve current and historical weather data, and flood incident history.\n{\n  \"agent\": \"flood_vulnerability_agent\",\n  \"query_id\": \"bellandur_flood_risk_tonight\",\n  \"timestamp\": \"2024-05-16T12:00:00Z\",\n  \"status\": \"success\",\n  \"confidence\": \"medium\",\n  \"data\": {\n    \"location\": \"Bellandur\",\n    \"flood_risk_factors\": [\n      \"Compound flood mechanism (Direct Pluvial + Bellandur lake backflow)\",\n      \"Rainfall intensity and duration\",\n      \"Bellandur lake fill levels\",\n      \"Antecedent rainfall conditions\"\n    ],\n    \"high_risk_locations\": [\n      \"Bellandur ORR Underpass\",\n      \"Agara Junction\",\n      \"Bellandur Main Road low-lying stretch (near State Bank Colony)\"\n    ],\n    \"rainfall_inundation_thresholds\": {\n      \"T1_inundation_onset\": {\n        \"lake_le_60pct\": \"18 mm/hr\",\n        \"lake_ge_70pct_watch\": \"14 mm/hr\",\n        \"lake_ge_82pct_warning\": \"10 mm/hr\",\n        \"lake_ge_92pct_danger\": \"6 mm/hr\"\n      },\n      \"T2_traffic_affected\": {\n        \"lake_le_60pct\": \"28 mm/hr\",\n        \"lake_ge_70pct_watch\": \"22 mm/hr\",\n        \"lake_ge_82pct_warning\": \"16 mm/hr\",\n        \"lake_ge_92pct_danger\": \"10 mm/hr\"\n      },\n {
```

```json
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "input_text",
        "text": "Here's an assessment of flood risk in Bellandur, based on available documentation. Please note that I was unable to retrieve real-time current weather or lake hydrology data due to a backend connectivity issue. Therefore, this assessment focuses on the established flood vulnerability patterns and thresholds for Bellandur.\n\nBellandur is highly susceptible to flooding primarily due to a compound flood mechanism: a combination of direct rainfall (pluvial) and backflow from Bellandur lake. This means that even moderate rainfall can lead to severe inundation, especially when Bellandur lake levels are high.\n\nHigh-Risk Locations in Bellandur:\n\nBellandur ORR Underpass: This is identified as a primary failure point due to its undersized box culvert and critically insufficient sump pump capacity. It is also directly impacted by Bellandur lake backflow.\nAgara Junction: A secondary failure point that typically experiences closure 15–20 minutes after the Bellandur ORR underpass.\nBellandur Main Road (near State Bank Colony): This low-lying stretch is prone to surface flooding, with typical depths of 30–45 cm during severe events.\nRainfall and Lake Level Thresholds for Inundation (for sustained rainfall for at least 20 minutes):\n\nThe likelihood and severity of flooding are significantly influenced by Bellandur lake's fill level. Here are the thresholds:\n\nSeverity\tBellandur Lake ≤ 60% Fill\tBellandur Lake ≥ 70% Fill (Watch)\tBellandur Lake ≥ 82% Fill (Warning)\tBellandur Lake ≥ 92% Fill (Danger)\nT1 (Inundation Onset)\t18 mm/hr\t14 mm/hr\t10 mm/hr\t6 mm/hr\nT2 (Traffic Affected)\t28 mm/hr\t22 mm/hr\t16 mm/hr\t10 mm/hr\nT3 (Closure Likely)\t38 mm/hr\t30 mm/hr\t22 mm/hr\t14 mm/hr\nImportant Considerations:\n\nLake Backflow Dominance: When Bellandur lake is above 94% fill, lake backflow is the primary driver of inundation, contributing 71–89% of observed flood depths. In such conditions, moderate sustained rainfall (e.g., 20 mm/hr for 60 minutes) can be more dangerous than intense but brief downpours.\nHysteresis Effect: Even after rainfall stops and lake levels begin to decline, the risk of backflow and inundation persists for 4–8 hours, impeding drainage from underpass sumps.\nAntecedent Rainfall: Prior rainfall increases flood risk. If 24-hour prior rainfall exceeds 50 mm, all thresholds are reduced by 15%. If 72-hour prior rainfall exceeds 120 mm, all thresholds are reduced by 25%.\nEarly Warning and Barricade Triggers:\n\nTo mitigate flood impact, specific triggers are recommended:\n\nBellandur ORR Underpass Barricade Trigger:\nRainfall intensity ≥ 32 mm/hr sustained for 20+ minutes, OR\nBellandur lake fill ≥ 85% AND rainfall ≥ 18 mm/hr, OR\nSump pump failure alarm is active.\nTime to road closure from trigger: 25–40 minutes.\nPre-emptive Barricading: If Bellandur lake fill exceeds 95%, pre-emptive barricading of the Bellandur ORR underpass and Agara underpass is recommended, regardless of current rainfall.\nIn summary, Bellandur's flood risk tonight would be determined by a combination of actual rainfall intensity, the current fill level of Bellandur lake, and any recent heavy rainfall. Without real-time data, it's not possible to provide a specific forecast for tonight. However, the documented thresholds highlight that even moderate rainfall can cause significant disruption if the lake is at high fill levels.\n\nConfidence: Medium (Due to lack of real-time data, but comprehensive understanding of vulnerability from documents). Data Freshness: Documents published between 2019 and 2022. Context Used:\n\nbangalore_flood_study_2022 (sections s2, s3, s5)\ndrainage_plan_bbmp_2019 (sections s2, s5) Citations:\nUrban Flood Vulnerability and Inundation Dynamics in Bangalore — A Technical Study (Indian Institute of Science (IISc) — Centre for Infrastructure, Sustainable Transportation and Urban Planning (CiSTUP), November 2022)\nBBMP Stormwater Drainage Master Plan — Bengaluru (Bruhat \"preemptive_barricading_condition\": \"Bellandur lake fill exceeds 95% regardless of current rainfall\"\n    },\n    \"realtime_data_unavailable\": true\n  },\n  \"citations\": [\n    {\n      \"publication\": \"Urban Flood Vulnerability and Inundation Dynamics in Bangalore \\u2014 A Technical Study\",\n      \"agency\": \"Indian Institute of Science (IISc) \\u2014 Centre for Infrastructure, Sustainable Transportation and Urban Planning (CiSTUP)\",\n      \"published_at\": \"2022-11\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"publication\": \"BBMP Stormwater Drainage Master Plan \\u2014 Bengaluru\",\n      \"agency\": \"Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP)\",\n      \"published_at\": \"2019-06\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"data_freshness\": \"Documents published between 2019 and 2022. Real-time data could not be retrieved.\",\n  \"context_used\": [\n    \"bangalore_flood_study_2022 (sections s2, s3, s5)\",\n    \"drainage_plan_bbmp_2019 (sections s2, s5)\"\n  ],\n  \"errors\": [\n    \"Backend HTTP errors were encountered when attempting to retrieve current and historical weather data for Bellandur.\",\n    \"Backend HTTP errors were encountered when attempting to retrieve flood incident history for Bellandur.\"\n  ]\n} so this is the response for a question schema is it becuse that all agents are running in prallel is that the reason why iam getting these answer all the agents are spun parallely so what iam thinking is a answer formatter agent that will get this response and intelligently format a answer to the question the user asked. ? do u want anything to confirm what is happening in the backend i used the ADK web to do so",
```
```json
    "images": [],
    "local_images": [],
    "text_elements": []
  }
}
```

## Entry 801
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T11:03:57.216Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T11:03:57.216Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "You’re seeing this mostly because of **execution path**, not just parallelism.\n\n- In **ADK Web**, you are talking to the ADK app directly, so it can surface raw/verbose agent output (including Level-1 JSON-like payloads).\n- The cleaner user response is supposed to happen in **backend `/api/chat`** where `agent_proxy` maps output into the Level-2 shape.\n\nSo yes, parallel fan-out can make payloads bigger, but the main reason this looks raw is that there’s no strict final formatting step in the ADK path you tested.\n\nAlso, your sample payload shows schema drift:\n- `status: \"success\"` (expected `ok | partial | error`)\n- `confidence: \"medium\"` (expected numeric)\nThat means prompt-only schema control is currently loose.\n\nI would **not add a separate formatter agent** yet. Better approach:\n1. Keep `chat_agent` as the final synthesizer.\n2. Enforce strict structured output (typed model/validator) before returning.\n3. Keep backend Level-2 mapping as final contract guard.\n\nTo confirm what’s happening, run the same query both ways:\n1. ADK Web (direct)\n2. Backend `POST /api/chat`\n\nIf backend response is clean and ADK Web is raw, then behavior is expected by path.  \nIf backend is also raw, then chat-agent final synthesis/validation is failing.\n\nIf you want, I can give you a quick debug checklist with exact commands/log points to isolate where formatting breaks (root -> chat -> backend mapping).",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

## Entry 804
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T11:03:57.541Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T11:03:57.541Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "task_complete",
    "turn_id": "019cdc90-e862-7ee2-b257-a4615c898630",
    "last_agent_message": "You’re seeing this mostly because of **execution path**, not just parallelism.\n\n- In **ADK Web**, you are talking to the ADK app directly, so it can surface raw/verbose agent output (including Level-1 JSON-like payloads).\n- The cleaner user response is supposed to happen in **backend `/api/chat`** where `agent_proxy` maps output into the Level-2 shape.\n\nSo yes, parallel fan-out can make payloads bigger, but the main reason this looks raw is that there’s no strict final formatting step in the ADK path you tested.\n\nAlso, your sample payload shows schema drift:\n- `status: \"success\"` (expected `ok | partial | error`)\n- `confidence: \"medium\"` (expected numeric)\nThat means prompt-only schema control is currently loose.\n\nI would **not add a separate formatter agent** yet. Better approach:\n1. Keep `chat_agent` as the final synthesizer.\n2. Enforce strict structured output (typed model/validator) before returning.\n3. Keep backend Level-2 mapping as final contract guard.\n\nTo confirm what’s happening, run the same query both ways:\n1. ADK Web (direct)\n2. Backend `POST /api/chat`\n\nIf backend response is clean and ADK Web is raw, then behavior is expected by path.  \nIf backend is also raw, then chat-agent final synthesis/validation is failing.\n\nIf you want, I can give you a quick debug checklist with exact commands/log points to isolate where formatting breaks (root -> chat -> backend mapping)."
  }
}
```

Better approach:\n1. Keep `chat_agent` as the final synthesizer.\n2. Enforce strict structured output (typed model/validator) before returning.\n3. Keep backend Level-2 mapping as final contract guard.\n\nTo confirm what’s happening, run the same query both ways:\n1. ADK Web (direct)\n2. Backend `POST /api/chat`\n\nIf backend response is clean and ADK Web is raw, then behavior is expected by path.  \nIf backend is also raw, then chat-agent final synthesis/validation is failing.\n\nIf you want, I can give you a quick debug checklist with exact commands/log points to isolate where formatting breaks (root -> chat -> backend mapping)."

## Entry 806
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T11:05:44.199Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T11:05:44.199Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc92-f406-7410-9929-004b6583df4a",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "realtime_active": false,
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Project Identity\n- This repository is the working MetroSense codebase: `client/` for React, `server/` for FastAPI, PostgreSQL via Docker Compose, and CI/quality gates.\n- The product is `MetroSense`, a chat-based climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru.\n- The target architecture is a three-service system:\n  - `client/`: frontend chat application\n  - `server/`: public backend API and orchestration layer\n  - `agents/`: internal agent server and tool-execution layer (✅ **exists and wired**, current status: skeleton with google_search tool only)\n- Treat the current implementation as the baseline and evolve it toward this three-service design instead of creating parallel app structures.\n- **Status**: ~40% complete. Auth flow, backend-to-agent routing, and database schema are functional. Data seeding, custom agent tools, risk cards/artifacts, streaming, and message persistence are planned.\n\n## Canonical Architecture\n- The intended request flow is:\n  - `frontend -> backend -> agent`\n- The frontend should never call the agent service directly.\n- `server/` is the only public API boundary for browser clients.\n- `agents/` is an internal service boundary responsible for agent runtime behavior, tool usage, and structured chat output generation.\n- If existing docs still use `agent/` singular, treat that as referring to the planned `agents/` subsystem until naming is aligned across the repo.\n\n## Why This Boundary Exists\n- Keep secrets, database access, internal tools, and agent infrastructure off the public internet.\n- Let the backend own request validation, session coordination, auth, rate limiting, logging, and response shaping.\n- Preserve a stable frontend contract even if the agent framework, prompting strategy, or toolchain changes.\n- Keep observability and failure handling centralized in the backend rather than split between browser and agent server.\n\n## Tech Stack\n- Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic, PostgreSQL 16, Loguru, `uv`\n- Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, MUI v6, React Query, Zustand, Axios, `pnpm`\n- Tooling: Ruff, MyPy strict, import-linter, ESLint, Docker, Docker Compose, GitHub Actions\n\n## Repository Shape\n- `client/`\n  - Chat UI, streaming presentation, structured response rendering, browser-facing state management.\n- `server/`\n  - Public HTTP API, request validation, orchestration, session handling, backend-to-agent coordination.\n- `agents/`\n  - Internal agent service for chat generation, tool calls, retrieval, and domain intelligence.\n- `server/app/api/`\n  - FastAPI routes and request/response boundaries.\n- `server/app/services/`\n  - Backend business logic and internal orchestration code.\n- `server/app/db/`\n  - Database models, metadata, and session management.\n- `server/tests/unit/`\n  - Fast isolated tests for backend logic and simple route behavior.\n- `server/tests/integration/`\n  - App-level and database-backed tests.\n- `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`\n  - Source of truth for the planned MetroSense chat UX and structured response behavior.\n\n## Architectural Rules\n- Preserve backend layering:\n  - `app.api` may depend on `app.services` and `app.core`\n  - `app.services` may depend on `app.db` and `app.core`\n  - `app.db` must not depend on `app.services` or `app.api`\n- Keep the frontend focused on rendering and interaction, not business orchestration.\n- Route all browser chat traffic through `server/`, even after `agents/` exists.\n- Treat backend-to-agent communication as an internal interface that may evolve without breaking the UI.\n- Prefer extending existing `server/` and `client/` entrypoints over introducing duplicate service layers or alternative public APIs.\n\n## Authentication\n- Public endpoints:\n  - `POST /api/auth/signup`\n  - `POST /api/auth/login`\n- Protected endpoints:\n  - All other `/api/*` routes require a valid JWT cookie.\n- `/health` remains public for uptime checks.\n\n## Product Direction\n- MetroSense is intended to be a chat-first application for weather risk, flooding, AQI, outage, traffic, and infrastructure-readiness questions.\n- Expected response modes:\n  - streamed assistant text for most responses\n  - agent-emitted `RiskCard` payloads for structured neighborhood risk outputs\n  - agent-emitted `Artifact` payloads for charts, maps, tables, or other visual responses\n- Structured outputs should be decided by the agent layer and delivered through the backend to the frontend.\n- The frontend must render structured responses based on explicit backend/agent event types, not keyword inference.\n\n## Testing And Validation\n- Backend checks from `server/`:\n  - `uv sync --all-extras`\n  - `uv run ruff format --check .`\n  - `uv run ruff check .`\n  - `uv run mypy .`\n  - `uv run lint-imports`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/unit -q`\n  - `uv run pytest tests/integration -q`\n- Agents checks from `agents/`:\n  - `uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

`uv sync`\n  - `uv run python scripts/smoke_test.py`\n- Frontend checks from `client/`:\n  - `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`\n  - `pnpm run lint`\n  - `pnpm run build`\n  - `pnpm exec playwright install`\n  - `pnpm run test:e2e`\n- Local startup today:\n  - The agents service runs behind a lightweight proxy that enforces the `X-Internal-Token` shared secret. The backend calls the proxy on port 8020, and the proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. `/health` stays public for simple availability checks.\n  - `docker-compose up -d db`\n  - `cd server && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run alembic upgrade head && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .`\n  - `cd agents && export AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token && uv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020`\n  - `cd agents && uv run adk web --port 8001` (optional UI)\n  - `cd client && pnpm install && pnpm run dev`\n- When expanding `agents/`, keep startup and validation commands updated in this file.\n\n## Current Coverage And Next-Step Expectations\n\n### Implemented (✅)\n- **Backend:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout/me with JWT + httponly cookies), health checks, database schema (9+ models for weather/AQI/flooding/outages/traffic), `/api/chat` endpoint\n- **Backend-to-Agent:** Proxy service with `X-Internal-Token` validation, session routing to ADK\n- **Frontend:** Chat UI (MessageBubble, MessageList, InputBar), auth context (login/signup/logout), RiskCard and ArtifactRenderer components, health polling\n- **Tests:** Auth flow (signup/login/logout), password hashing, health checks, basic chat endpoint\n\n### Planned (📋)\n- **Agent Tools:** Custom tools for flood-risk assessment, AQI lookup, outage status, traffic conditions, document retrieval\n- **Data Seeding:** Load CSV data (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) into database\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts emitted by agent layer; backend extracts and shapes for frontend\n- **Streaming:** Server-sent events (SSE) or WebSocket for streamed text responses\n- **Message Persistence:** Store chat history in database; load conversation context for agents\n- **Chat Response Tests:** Extend coverage for agent output parsing, risk card generation, artifact rendering\n\n### Current Test Coverage\n- Backend: Health, auth (signup/login/logout/me), password hashing, basic chat routing\n- Frontend: Component renders (no E2E tests written yet)\n- Agents: smoke_test.py validates proxy routing only (not domain logic)\n\n### Next Steps (Recommended Order)\n1. Implement data loader to seed MetroSense CSVs and documents\n2. Add custom agent tools (flood-risk, aqi-lookup, outage-status, traffic)\n3. Implement structured response generation (risk cards/artifacts from agent)\n4. Add message history persistence and context retrieval\n5. Implement streaming responses (SSE or WebSocket)\n6. Extend test coverage for full end-to-end flows\n\n## Agent Guidance\n- Before major implementation work, verify repo truth from `README.md`, `server/pyproject.toml`, `client/package.json`, and `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`.\n- Prefer `rg` for search.\n- Do not document or reference unimplemented services as if they already exist.\n- **Important:** The CSV data files (DataSet_MetroSense/) and documents (Documents_Metrosense/) exist in the repo but are **not yet loaded** into the database. Implement a data loader before building agent tools that depend on MetroSense domain knowledge.\n- The agent service skeleton runs and routes to google_search tool, but custom domain tools (flood-risk, aqi, outage, traffic) do not yet exist. Add tools incrementally as matching backend data becomes available.\n- If current behavior and MetroSense direction diverge, align new work to the MetroSense direction while keeping transitions explicit and incremental.\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- google-adk-python: Expert guidance on the Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Python. Use this skill when the user asks about building agents, using tools, streaming, callbacks, tutorials, deployment, or advanced architecture with the Google ADK in Python. (file: /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense/.agents/skills/google-adk-python/SKILL.md)\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
```json
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

```json
    "output": "Chunk ID: ee439c\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2714\nOutput:\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport argparse\nimport csv\nimport json\nimport sys\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\nfrom zoneinfo import ZoneInfo\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import text\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncConnection, create_async_engine\n\n# Allow direct script execution via:\n# `uv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace`\nif __package__ is None or __package__ == \"\":\n    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]))\n\nfrom app.core.config import SERVER_DIR, get_settings\n\nDATASET_DIR = SERVER_DIR / \"DataSet_MetroSense\"\nDOCUMENTS_DIR = SERVER_DIR / \"Documents_Metrosense\"\nIST = ZoneInfo(\"Asia/Kolkata\")\n\nDATASET_TABLE_ORDER = [\n    \"air_quality_observation\",\n    \"weather_observation\",\n    \"flood_incident\",\n    \"power_outage_event\",\n    \"traffic_segment_observation\",\n    \"lake_hydrology\",\n    \"ward_profile\",\n    \"lake_reference\",\n    \"location_master\",\n]\n\nCSV_TABLES = {\n    \"air_quality_observations.csv\": \"air_quality_observation\",\n    \"weather_observations.csv\": \"weather_observation\",\n    \"flood_incidents.csv\": \"flood_incident\",\n    \"power_outage_events.csv\": \"power_outage_event\",\n    \"traffic_segment_observations.csv\": \"traffic_segment_observation\",\n    \"lake_hydrology.csv\": \"lake_hydrology\",\n}\n\nLAKE_NAME_BY_ID = {\n    \"lake_001\": \"Bellandur\",\n    \"lake_002\": \"Varthur\",\n    \"lake_003\": \"Hebbal\",\n    \"lake_004\": \"Nagavara\",\n    \"lake_005\": \"KR Puram\",\n    \"lake_006\": \"Madiwala\",\n}\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass FactReferences:\n    location_ids: set[str]\n    ward_ids: set[str]\n    lake_ids: set[str]\n\n\nclass LoaderValidationError(RuntimeError):\n    pass\n\n\ndef _parse_timestamp(value: str | None) -> datetime | None:\n    if not value:\n        return None\n    dt = datetime.fromisoformat(value)\n    if dt.tzinfo is None:\n        return dt.replace(tzinfo=IST)\n    return dt\n\n\ndef _to_bool(value: str | None) -> bool | None:\n    if value is None or value == \"\":\n        return None\n    low = value.lower()\n    if low == \"true\":\n        return True\n    if low == \"false\":\n        return False\n    raise LoaderValidationError(f\"Unexpected boolean literal: {value}\")\n\n\ndef _to_float(value: str | None) -> float | None:\n    if value is None or value == \"\":\n        return None\n    return float(value)\n\n\ndef _to_int(value: str | None) -> int | None:\n    if value is None or value == \"\":\n        return None\n    return int(value)\n\n\ndef _norm(value: str) -> str:\n    return \" \".join(value.strip().split()).lower()\n\n\ndef _display_name_from_zone(zone_id: str) -> str:\n    if not zone_id.startswith(\"zone_\"):\n        return zone_id\n    return zone_id.replace(\"zone_\", \"\").replace(\"_\", \" \").title()\n\n\ndef _load_document_names() -> set[str]:\n    names: set[str] = set()\n    for index_file in DOCUMENTS_DIR.glob(\"*_index.json\"):\n        payload = json.loads(index_file.read_text())\n        names.update(payload.get(\"wards_covered\", []))\n        for section in payload.get(\"table_of_contents\", []):\n            names.update(section.get(\"wards_mentioned\", []))\n    return names\n\n\ndef _collect_references() -> tuple[FactReferences, dict[str, str], dict[str, str]]:\n    location_ids: set[str] = set()\n    ward_ids: set[str] = set()\n    lake_ids: set[str] = set()\n    ward_name_by_id: dict[str, str] = {}\n    zone_name_by_ward_id: dict[str, str] = {}\n\n    for csv_name in CSV_TABLES:\n        with (DATASET_DIR / csv_name).open(newline=\"\") as handle:\n            reader = csv.DictReader(handle)\n            for row in reader:\n                location = (row.get(\"location_id\") or \"\").strip()\n                if location:\n                    location_ids.add(location)\n\n                ward = (row.get(\"ward_id\") or \"\").strip()\n                if ward:\n                    ward_ids.add(ward)\n\n                lake = (row.get(\"lake_id\") or \"\").strip()\n                if lake:\n                    lake_ids.add(lake)\n\n                if csv_name == \"air_quality_observations.csv\":\n                    if ward and location and not ward.startswith(\"zone_\"):\n                        ward_name_by_id[ward] = location\n\n                if csv_name == \"flood_incidents.csv\":\n                    if ward and (row.get(\"neighborhood_name\") or \"\").strip():\n                        ward_name_by_id.setdefault(ward, row[\"neighborhood_name\"].strip())\n\n                if csv_name == \"power_outage_events.csv\":\n                    if ward and (row.get(\"zone_name\") or \"\").strip():\n                        zone_name_by_ward_id[ward] = row[\"zone_name\"].strip()\n\n    return (\n        FactReferences(location_ids=location_ids, ward_ids=ward_ids, lake_ids=lake_ids),\n        ward_name_by_id,\n        zone_name_by_ward_id,\n    )\n\n\ndef _build_location_rows(\n    refs: FactReferences,\n    ward_name_by_id: dict[str, str],\n    zone_name_by_ward_id: dict[str, str],\n) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    now = datetime.now().astimezone()\n    all_known_names = _load_document_names()\n    canonical_name_by_norm = {_norm(name): name for name in all_known_names}\n    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n\n    for ward_id in sorted(refs.ward_ids):\n        ward_name = ward_name_by_id.get(ward_id)\n        zone_name = zone_name_by_ward_id.get(ward_id)\n        canonical = ward_name or ward_id\n        alias_set = {canonical, ward_id.replace(\"_\", \" \")}\n        rows.append(\n            {\n                \"location_id\": ward_id,\n                \"canonical_name\": canonical,\n                \"location_type\": \"ward\",\n                \"aliases\": sorted(alias_set),\n                \"ward_id\": ward_id,\n                \"ward_name\": ward_name,\n                \"zone_name\": zone_name,\n                \"neighborhood_name\": ward_name,\n                \"pin_code\": None,\n                \"latitude\": None,\n                \"longitude\": None,\n                \"geometry\": None,\n                \"created_at\": now,\n                \"updated_at\": now,\n            }\n        )\n\n    for location_id in sorted(refs.location_ids):\n        if location_id in refs.ward_ids:\n            continue\n\n        loc_ward_id: str | None = None\n        loc_ward_name: str | None = None\n        loc_zone_name: str | None = None\n        loc_neighborhood_name: str | None = None\n\n        if location_id.startswith(\"zone_\"):\n            canonical = _display_name_from_zone(location_id)\n            loc_type = \"zone\"\n            loc_zone_name = location_id\n        else:\n            normalized = _norm(location_id)\n            canonical = canonical_name_by_norm.get(normalized, location_id)\n            loc_type = \"neighborhood\"\n            loc_neighborhood_name = canonical\n\n        rows.append(\n            {\n                \"location_id\": location_id,\n                \"canonical_name\": canonical,\n                \"location_type\": loc_type,\n                \"aliases\": sorted({canonical, location_id}),\n                \"ward_id\": loc_ward_id,\n                \"ward_name\": loc_ward_name,\n                \"zone_name\": loc_zone_name,\n                \"neighborhood_name\": loc_neighborhood_name,\n                \"pin_code\": None,\n                \"latitude\": None,\n                \"longitude\": None,\n                \"geometry\": None,\n                \"created_at\": now,\n                \"updated_at\": now,\n            }\n        )\n\n    return rows\n\n\ndef _build_ward_profile_rows(ward_ids: set[str]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    return [\n        {\n            \"profile_id\": f\"profile_{ward_id}\",\n            \"ward_id\": ward_id,\n            \"population_density\": None,\n            \"elderly_population_pct\": None,\n            \"children_population_pct\": None,\n            \"slum_household_count\": None,\n            \"green_cover_pct\": None,\n            \"impervious_surface_pct\": None,\n            \"data_year\": None,\n            \"source_name\": \"Documents_Metrosense\",\n        }\n        for ward_id in sorted(ward_ids)\n    ]\n\n\ndef _build_lake_reference_rows(\n    lake_ids: set[str],\n    known_location_ids: set[str],\n) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:\n    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []\n    for lake_id in sorted(lake_ids):\n        lake_name = LAKE_NAME_BY_ID.get(lake_id, lake_id)\n        rows.append(\n            {\n                \"lake_id\": lake_id,\n                \"lake_name\": lake_name,\n                \"lake_type\": None,\n                \"location_id\": lake_name if lake_name in known_location_ids else None,\n                \"ward_id\": None,\n                \"latitude\": None,\n                \"longitude\": None,\n                \"catchment_area_sqkm\": None,\n                \"lake_area_sqkm\": None,\n                \"full_tank_level_meters\": None,\n                \"max_capacity_mcm\": None,\n                \"number_of_gates\": None,\n                \"source_name\": \"Documents_Metrosense\",\n            }\n        )\n    return rows\n\n\ndef _validate_location_coverage(location_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], refs: FactReferences) -> None:\n    canonical_ids = {row[\"location_id\"] for row in location_rows}\n    missing_locations = sorted(refs.location_ids - canonical_ids)\n    missing_wards = sorted(refs.ward_ids - canonical_ids)\n\n    if missing_locations or missing_wards:\n        detail = {\n            \"missing_location_ids\": missing_locations[:20],\n            \"missing_ward_ids\": missing_wards[:20],\n            \"missing_location_count\": len(missing_locations),\n            \"missing_ward_count\": len(missing_wards),\n        }\n        raise LoaderValidationError(\n            \"Location normalization failed coverage check: \" + json.dumps(detail, sort_keys=True)\n        )\n\n\ndef _transform_row(table_name: str, row: dict[str, str]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    transformed: dict[str, Any] = {\n        key: (value if value != \"\" else None) for key, value in row.items()\n    }\n\n    if table_name == \"weather_observation\":\n        transformed[\"observed_at\"] = _parse_timestamp(row[\"observed_at\"])\n        transformed[\"ingested_at\"] = _parse_timestamp(row[\"ingested_at\"])\n        transformed[\"elevation_meters\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"elevation_meters\"))\n        transformed[\"temperature_celsius\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"temperature_celsius\"))\n        transformed[\"humidity_percent\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"humidity_percent\"))\n        transformed[\"pressure_hpa\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"pressure_hpa\"))\n        transformed[\"wind_speed_kmh\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"wind_speed_kmh\"))\n        transformed[\"wind_gust_kmh\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"wind_gust_kmh\"))\n        transformed[\"wind_direction_degrees\"] = _to_int(row.get(\"wind_direction_degrees\"))\n        transformed[\"rainfall_mm_hourly\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"rainfall_mm_hourly\"))\n        transformed[\"rainfall_mm_24hour\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"rainfall_mm_24hour\"))\n        transformed[\"rainfall_intensity_mm_hr\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"rainfall_intensity_mm_hr\"))\n        transformed[\"visibility_km\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"visibility_km\"))\n        transformed[\"latitude\"] = _to_float(row.get(\"latitude\"))\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 824
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T11:06:08.914Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T11:06:08.914Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense && python3 - <<'PY'\\nimport csv,glob\\nfrom datetime import datetime\\n\\nfiles = {\\n 'air_quality_observations.csv':'observed_at',\\n 'weather_observations.csv':'observed_at',\\n 'flood_incidents.csv':'reported_at',\\n 'power_outage_events.csv':'started_at',\\n 'traffic_segment_observations.csv':'observed_at',\\n 'lake_hydrology.csv':'observed_at',\\n}\\nfor path in sorted(glob.glob('server/DataSet_MetroSense/*.csv')):\\n    name=path.split('/')[-1]\\n    col=files.get(name)\\n    with open(path,newline='') as f:\\n        r=csv.DictReader(f)\\n        n=0\\n        mn=mx=None\\n        for row in r:\\n            n+=1\\n            if col:\\n                v=row[col].replace('T',' ')\\n                try:\\n                    dt=datetime.fromisoformat(v)\\n                except Exception:\\n                    continue\\n                mn=dt if mn is None or dt<mn else mn\\n                mx=dt if mx is None or dt>mx else mx\\n        print(f\\\"{name}: rows={n}, {col}_min={mn}, {col}_max={mx}\\\")\\nPY\"}",
    "call_id": "call_CIr8uSVab8cluNRlUAQH4GGe"
  }
}
```

```json
}
```

## Entry 828
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T11:06:21.211Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T11:06:21.211Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cd /home/dell/ai-track-metrosense && python3 - <<'PY'\\nimport csv,glob\\nloc=set(); ward=set(); lake=set();\\nfor p in glob.glob('server/DataSet_MetroSense/*.csv'):\\n    with open(p,newline='') as f:\\n        r=csv.DictReader(f)\\n        for row in r:\\n            if row.get('location_id'): loc.add(row['location_id'].strip())\\n            if row.get('ward_id'): ward.add(row['ward_id'].strip())\\n            if row.get('lake_id'): lake.add(row['lake_id'].strip())\\nprint('unique location_id',len([x for x in loc if x]))\\nprint('unique ward_id',len([x for x in ward if x]))\\nprint('unique lake_id',len([x for x in lake if x]))\\nprint('sample locations',sorted([x for x in loc if x])[:12])\\nprint('sample wards',sorted([x for x in ward if x])[:12])\\nprint('lakes',sorted([x for x in lake if x]))\\nPY\"}",
    "call_id": "call_iUI5tbpIjvbCXa1R1BHR54FA"
  }
}
```

```json
}
```

## Entry 832
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T11:06:42.539Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T11:06:42.539Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Good catch. Based on the actual seed files in this repo, the seeded telemetry is **2023-only**, not 2022.\n\n**What is in `server/DataSet_MetroSense/`:**\n- `weather_observations.csv`: **175,200 rows**, `observed_at` from **2023-01-01 00:00:00** to **2023-12-31 23:45:00**\n- `air_quality_observations.csv`: **560,640 rows**, `observed_at` from **2023-01-01 00:00:00** to **2023-12-31 23:45:00**\n- `traffic_segment_observations.csv`: **52,560 rows**, `observed_at` from **2023-01-01 00:00:00** to **2023-12-31 23:00:00**\n- `lake_hydrology.csv`: **8,760 rows**, `observed_at` from **2023-01-01 00:00:00** to **2023-12-31 18:00:00**\n- `power_outage_events.csv`: **671 rows**, `started_at` from **2023-01-01 14:21:00** to **2023-12-31 21:41:00**\n- `flood_incidents.csv`: **74 rows**, `reported_at` from **2023-06-01 02:00:00** to **2023-10-30 05:00:00**\n\n**Reference coverage inferred from seed:**\n- ~29 `location_id`s\n- 16 wards (`ward_001` … `ward_016`)\n- 6 lakes (`lake_001` … `lake_006`)\n\nSo if you asked for **Bellandur historical weather in April 2022**, there is no seeded 2022 weather data to return.  \nThat part should ideally surface as **NO_DATA_AVAILABLE**, not just “backend connectivity issue.”\n\nIf you want, I can give you one SQL/checklist to confirm what’s currently loaded in your running DB (in case loader hasn’t been run or data differs from files).",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T11:16:04.964Z",

  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cdc9c-6ce2-70c2-8d55-f5a16a8e60ad",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/ai-track-metrosense",
    "current_date": "2026-03-11",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "writable_roots": [
        "/home/dell/.codex/memories"
      ],

      "mode": "default",
```
